articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Log in to your learner account.

Overview:

Your EzLicence learner account gives you the ability to easily:

  • Make new bookings
  • Cancel or reschedule your existing bookings
  • View your purchase history
  • View your available credits
  • Change your preferences - default address, transmission
  • Change instructors
  • Rate your instructor

If you are having trouble logging in, click here to troubleshoot.

How to log in to your learner account

Step 1.

From our website, in the top right hand side of your screen, click the Menu icon. 

Step 2.

Select Learner Login.

Step 3.

Enter the email address and password you used when you created your account.

Step 4.

Click Login.

You will automatically land on your Dashboard.

articles.title: How to fix a payment error
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Fix a payment error where payment...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Fix a payment error where payment has been made multiple times.

Overview:

Sometimes, we get a little excited and accidentally pay multiple times, literally within minutes of each other. We’ve been there.

We can offer 2 options:

  1. You can keep the extra credit and redeem it when it suits you. The credit is valid for 24 months; or
  2. We can process a refund back to your source of payment, this can take 5-10 business days, depending on the bank processing time.

If you are happy to keep the credit, no further action is required. If you would prefer a refund, please submit a support request to our team for processing.

articles.title: How to find an instructor where there is no availability
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Find a driving instructor near...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Find a driving instructor near you, the website is saying there is no availability.

Overview:

The EzLicence booking platform operates based on suburbs. When you use the search tool on our website, it is best to enter your pick up suburb.

If your search comes up with no results, it is because there are no driving instructors currently servicing your pick-up suburb. In this scenario there are a couple of options:

  • Try a neighbouring suburb
  • Contact our team

Try a neighbouring area

We recommend searching for your surrounding areas or perhaps the suburb of a location you may be willing to travel to in order to have driving lessons.

Remember that if a driving instructor comes up in another area and you wish to book with them, you will need to meet them there. Often, learners will meet their driving instructor at a train station, shopping centre, McDonalds or a test centre. During the booking process, when asked for your pick-up address, you will need to enter the meeting point address.

Ready to book a driving lesson? Search for a driving instructor now!

Contact us

If you are struggling to find an instructor via our website, you can submit a request.

articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Change the bank account...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Change the bank account that EzLicence makes payments to.

Overview:

EzLicence makes payments to driving instructors on a fortnightly basis, with payments processed on a Monday. Depending on your financial institution, you may receive this payment the same day or the following day. Keep in mind, when payday falls on a Public Holiday and banks are closed, your payment will be delayed.

Each payout cycle will include all authorised payments from Monday through to the following Sunday. You can click here for more information on how to process your payments.

How to change your bank account details

At this stage, driving instructors are unable to change their bank account details via their instructor account.

Please submit a support request and our team will make the required changes. In order for us to action your request, please provide us with following information:

  1. Account Name
  2. BSB Number
  3. Account Number

articles.title: How to request a late cancellation with your instructor (Less than 24 hrs)
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform You are trying to:  Cancel a booking with your driving...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform

You are trying to: 

Cancel a booking with your driving instructor less than 24 hours beforehand

Overview:

Unfortunately, there are times when you need to cancel a booking at the last minute, some situations can't be avoided. Some examples are:

  • You are unwell
  • Someone in your immediate family is unwell and you need to provide care
  • You’ve been called into work

If you are cancelling within 24 hours of the booking start time, your cancellation will count as a late cancellation and you will be charged the full booking fee, to compensate your instructor for lost income and time.

How to request a late cancellation with your instructor (Less than 24 hrs)

Step 1.

Log in to your learner account.

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, scroll down to Bookings

Step 3.

Select Upcoming from the dropdown.

Step 4.

Locate the booking and click the Booking #

Step 5.

Click Modify Booking.

Step 6.

Click Cancel (Late Cancellation).

Step 7.

A confirmation box will appear advising you of the late cancellation and that cancellation fees apply.

Step 8.

Click Reason for cancellation and select the appropriate option.

Step 9.

In the text box, type a message for your driving instructor providing any additional information.

Step 10.

Check the box next to "I understand and agree to the Cancellation Policy".

Step 11.

Click Cancel Booking.

Step 12.

You will be taken back to your Dashboard, where your booking will be displayed under Booking History. The status will be marked as Late Cancellation and Payment marked as Processed.

Important

During the cancellation process, you will be asked to provide a reason for the cancellation which will be shared with your driving instructor. Where applicable, your driving instructor has 30 days to reverse this cancellation fee. 

If a payment is reversed by your driving instructor, you will be notified via email. The booking history will then show the payment status as Reversed. For further information, refer to our support article - How to view your booking history.

articles.title: How to reverse a payment
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Reverse a payment and...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Reverse a payment and not charge the learner for a cancelled booking.

Overview:

We understand that life can be unpredictable, and sometimes unforeseen circumstances can prevent you or your learner from keeping your booking. EzLicence’s goal is to ensure a smooth and fair experience for everyone.  Below are examples of situations where you may choose to reverse a payment and not charge for a booking:

1.    Medical Emergency:

When a learner or their immediate family member experiences a sudden illness or medical emergency, or the learner is deemed contagious by a medical professional.

2.    Traffic Issue:

When there are significant traffic issues due to road closures or car accidents, and a driving lesson is not possible.

3.    Natural Disaster:

Severe weather events, earthquakes, floods, or other natural disasters prevent safe driving conditions for a learner driver.

4.    Bereavement:

If there has been a recent death in the immediate family.

5.    Pandemic or Public Health Orders:

If public health orders or guidelines due to a pandemic or other public health crisis prevent a driving lesson from going ahead.

We recommend actioning any reversals as soon as practical, this will ensure a smooth experience for your learner. You do have up to 30 days to reverse a payment if required.

How to reverse a payment

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From your Dashboard, scroll down to Upcoming Bookings.

Step 3.

Click the Booking History tab.

Step 4.

Locate the booking in this list, you may need to move to the next page.

Step 5. 

Click Booking # to open the booking details.

Step 6. 

In the payment section, click Reverse Payment.

Step 7. 

Using the dropdown, select a Reason for the reversal.

Step 8.

In the text box below, type a Message for Learner. Take a moment to re-read your message as this cannot be undone.

Step 9.

Check the box next to "I understand and agree to the EzLicence Payment Policy"

Step 10.

Click Reverse Payment.

Step 11.

The booking will now appear in your Bookings History with a payment status of Returned.

articles.title: How to update your accreditation
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Update your...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Update your accreditation documents.

Overview:

It is important to keep the accreditation documents on your EzLicence profile up to date. These accreditation documents include:

  • Driver’s Licence
  • Driving Instructor’s Licence
  • Working With Children / Blue Card

As soon as one of these documents expire, your calendar will be closed to all bookings and appear as booked out to learners until we receive a copy of your updated accreditation document

To ensure you have ample time to update any documents that are coming up for expiry, EzLicence will send you a series of reminder emails which you will receive:

  • 90 days before expiry
  • 60 days before expiry
  • 30 days before expiry
  • 14 days before expiry 

You can submit your accreditation document(s) to us in a few quick steps. Ahead of submitting, please note the document requirements: 

  • Image must be clear, with all edges included
  • Image must be in colour
  • Image must be in .png or .jpg format
  • Image must be at least of 500 pixels wide

How to update your accreditations

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, find the Settings Icon and select Documents. 

 

Step 3.

From the Documents page, select the ↑submit new document button next to the item you wish to update.

Step 4.

You will then need to upload a photo of your new document, each document will have different requirements.

Step 5.

Once you have uploaded your photo, please enter the Expiration Date.

Step 6.

Once complete, click the Submit For Review button.

Important

Once completed, it will come to our team for review. Once verified, the document will be approved and your calendar will be opened.

If you have received an INTERIM licence, you can submit this in the same way. Our team will temporarily extend your calendar until you receive your new document. When you do, please update it in the same way.

If you need any help, please submit a request

articles.title: Review Policy
articles.excerpt: Reviews are one of the ways EzLicence fosters trust between learners and instructors. It helps learners make informed decisions and...
articles.content:
Reviews are one of the ways EzLicence fosters trust between learners and instructors. It helps learners make informed decisions and provides instructors with honest feedback to help them improve. Our reviews policy is intended to help ensure the feedback provided through our platform is authentic, trustworthy, and useful to our learners and instructors.  

Reviews should be unbiased

  • Instructors, Learners & parents should not coerce, intimidate, extort, threaten, incentivize or manipulate another person in an attempt to influence a review, like promising compensation in exchange for a positive review or threatening consequences in the event of a negative review.
  • Reviews may not be provided or withheld in exchange for something of value - like a discount, refund, reciprocal review, or promise not to take negative action against the reviewer. They also may not be used as an attempt to mislead or deceive EzLicence or another person. For example, learners should not write biassed or inauthentic reviews as a form of retaliation against an Instructor who enforces a policy or rule.
  • Reviews may only be provided in connection with a genuine lesson. For example, Instructors are not allowed to accept a fake booking in exchange for a positive review, use a second account to leave themselves a review, or coordinate with others to manipulate the review system.

Reviews should be relevant

  • Reviews must provide relevant information about the reviewer’s experience with the Instructor that would help other learners make an informed decision.

Reviews should not contain explicit, discriminatory, harmful, fraudulent or illegal content.

Reviews posted more than 6 months after service use

To ensure the accuracy and relevance of published feedback, reviews may be removed if they are submitted more than 6 months after the customer's last use of our services. Due to significant time lapse, such reviews may no longer provide a current or direct reflection of the booking experience. This policy helps maintain the integrity of reviews presented to other customers.

Reporting reviews under this policy

To report a review that violates this policy, contact us.

Removing a review you wrote

Once a review you have written has been published, you can contact us if you wish for it to be removed or amended.

articles.title: How to delivering a positive lesson experience
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Improve your business...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Improve your business by ensuring you are delivering a positive learner experience.

Overview:

Want to deliver the best possible experience for your learners? We’ve spoken with our most successful instructor partners to bring you their top tips for delivering excellent driving lessons and having happy learners.

Punctuality

The number one tip by highly successful driving instructors is to turn up on time for bookings and if you are running late, for example, due to traffic or other unforeseen events, early communication to the learner is critical and advise them of your expected arrival time. 

Communicate 

It is important to have open communication with your learners both before and during the booking. 

During the booking, offer constructive feedback to your learner using positive language. This will encourage them to learn and grow as a driver.

Seek feedback

The best instructors are always looking for ways to improve their teaching methods.  One way to do this is to ask your learners for feedback. Gaining this type of feedback will help you process it quickly and make necessary improvements. 

Request a review 

Each learner who has completed driving lessons with you is able to provide a review. Encourage your learners to provide a review of your teaching by asking them at the end of their first lesson. Our support article can assist them in completing the review process.  

Code of Conduct

The EzLicence Driving Instructor Code of Conduct sets a framework for professionalism across our network. Understanding and adhering to the Code of Conduct will ensure you are presenting yourself and representing EzLicence in the best possible way. 


If you have any questions about this, or would like to chat further about how to improve your rating, please contact us.

articles.title: How to maximise your bookings
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.You are trying to: Maximise the number of...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Maximise the number of bookings in your calendar.

Overview:

EzLicence uses Smart Scheduling to maximise the booking times in your calendar. It works by first, considering your operating hours and travel time, then it generates fixed booking times for learners. These booking times adjust, in real-time, as both EzLicence bookings and your private events are added to the calendar. As a result, the maximum number of bookings can be received each day. It is automatically applied to every Driving Instructor’s account.

To maximise the benefits of Smart Scheduling, we encourage driving instructors to keep the "Smart Scheduling View'' switched on. When it is turned on, you will see the same available lesson options learners will see. In addition to this, you can use these times as a guide when you are sending a Booking Proposal or adding a Personal Event. This will minimise any non-bookable time gaps in the day.

How to maximise your bookings

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar Settings

Step 3.

Scroll down until you see Smart Scheduling 

Click the toggle to "on", the default view will be 1 hr bookings. The calendar will show recommended booking times in green. You can also select to view 2 hr bookings

Things to Consider

  • Smart Scheduling only applies to 1-hour and 2-hour lessons
  • Test Packages can still be booked in 15-minute increments
  • Instructors can still send proposals and add personal events in 15-minute increments as needed.

articles.title: Just a moment...
How to understand the pricing ranges
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform You are trying to:  Understand the pricing ranges....
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform

You are trying to: 

Understand the pricing ranges.

Overview:

The EzLicence booking platform allows you to easily find, compare and book a driving instructor in your area. Simply use the searching tool to enter your pick up suburb and transmission type. 

Once you have completed the search, you will be presented with a pricing table which summarises the prices for the listed driving instructors. The pricing table will show you the lowest price per hour for your suburb and each individual instructor profile will show the range that applies to them. All of our Driving Instructor partners are independent business operators who set their own price.

EzLicence offers discounts when purchasing 6 hrs or more of driving lessons in one transaction. These discounts are shown in the pricing table and apply to all driving instructors. Discounts will automatically be applied to your cart when purchasing.

Let’s dive in and take a closer look!

How to understand the pricing ranges

Step 1.

From the EzLicence homepage, enter your pick-up suburb in the search tool and click search.

The pricing table will appear at the top of the page, each Driving Instructor’s profile will display their price range.

Step 2.

You can view more information about each Driving Instructor by clicking View Profile. 

On each Driving Instructor’s profile page, their personal pricing information is displayed. 

To help you choose the right Driving Instructor for you, we recommend the following:

  • Reading their bio 
  • View their vehicle information
  • Their overall rating and their most recent learner reviews
  • Their service area map, you can be picked up and dropped off within this area. 
  • View their real-time availability to ensure they are available at times that fit in with your schedule

Important

Remember that we offer driving lessons across Australia and in most major cities with market leading booking choice and flexibility:

  1. You are able to reschedule or cancel any booking online up to 24 hrs prior to the booking start time.
  2. You are able to change your driving instructor selection at any time. We provide access to the largest selection of driving instructors in Australia. 
  3. If you change Driving Instructors, when it comes to buying more lesson credits, you will need to purchase them at your new Driving Instructor’s price.

articles.content:
Parties

This Agreement is between –

  1. EzLicence; and
  2. the Learner who has signed up to the EzLicence platform in accordance with these Terms and Conditions.

OPERATIVE PROVISIONS

Term of Agreement

1.   This Agreement commences on the day the last party accepts this Agreement digitally and terminates when an Event of Termination takes place.

Capacity

2.   The Learner warrants and agrees that they have a current and valid Learner Driving Permit, or any other driver’s licence type that legally allows them to drive in the state or territory in which they are undertaking driving instruction with Driving Instructors.

3.   This Agreement automatically terminates if a Learner ceases to hold or is found not to have a valid current or Learner Driving Permit or another driver’s licence type that has not been suspended and that lawfully allows them to drive. This is considered an essential term by EzLicence.

Relationship of the Parties

4.   The Learner acknowledges and agrees that –

4.1.  they are directly engaging a Driving Instructor, who is at all times acting as an independent contractor to schedule, arrange and give driving instruction services;

4.2.  EzLicence, and the EzLicence website, is the conduit between Driving Instructors and Learners;

4.3.  any agreement to undertake driving lessons is between the Learner and the independent Driving Instructor, to which EzLicence is not a party;

4.4.  the Driving Instructor is independent of EzLicence and is not an agent, employee, contractor or subcontractor of EzLicence, and EzLicence is not liable or responsible for the conduct, acts or omissions of the Driving Instructors; and

4.5.  EzLicence does not itself check or carry out searches or due diligence on Driving Instructors. Learners are always responsible for and obliged to take all reasonable precautions in relation to their personal and property safety and security and EzLicence is not responsible or liable for any physical, mental or emotional loss, claim, harm or damage suffered as a result or in connection with, or consequence of booking or attending a lesson with a Driving Instructor.

Description of Services

5.   A Learner agrees that they may use the EzLicence website in a variety of ways, including but not limited to –

5.1.  indicating their personal preferences on the EzLicence website; and

5.2.  browsing a selection of Driving Instructors on the EzLicence website.

6.   A Learner can compare Driving Instructors by, including but not limited to –

6.1.  experience;

6.2.  price

6.3.  vehicle details;

6.4.  availability;

6.5.  ratings; and

6.6.  location.

7.   A Learner may select a Driving Instructor based on their preferences.

8.   EzLicence may add new products, change existing products or remove products from the EzLicence website as required from time to time.

Agreement by Learner

9.   The Learner warrants and agrees that they have –

9.1.  read and understood and consent to be bound by this Agreement; and

9.2.  a current and valid Learner Driving Permit or other licence type that legally allows them to drive in their state or territory pursuant to clause 2.

10.   If you are the parent or guardian of a Learner aged 17 years or younger, you are the financial guarantor of the Learner for their liabilities and obligations as set out in this Agreement and this Agreement binds you and the Learner jointly and severally.

11.   If you are the agent or service provider or person acting on behalf of, a Learner aged 18 years or older you guarantee that the Learner consents to be bound by this Agreement according to clause 9.1 above, and this Agreement binds you and the Learner jointly and severally.

Learner’s Use of EzLicence Website

12.   The Learner agrees to the following conditions –

12.1.  to use their own username and password, and not to ‘borrow’ another user’s details, to access and use the EzLicence website;

12.2.  not to divulge their username or password to any one or allow others to use it on their behalf;

12.3.  only to use the EzLicence website for its intended purpose to connect Driving Instructors with Learners, and in particular the Learner agrees that EzLicence is –

12.3.1.  a third party provider and acts as a matching service between Driving Instructors and Learners; and

12.3.2.  not responsible for the accuracy of a Driving Instructor’s information displayed on the EzLicence website;

12.4.  that information they have provided is current, true and correct;

12.5.  to amend their details as soon as their circumstances change;

12.6.  not to use the EzLicence platform except for as provided for in this Agreement, including not to disseminate hate speech or similar views or any other illegal activities or purposes;

12.7.  not to use the EzLicence website to access other accounts, data or information from other EzLicence website users;

12.8.  not to use the EzLicence platform to promote any third parties or other commercial interests; and

12.9.  not to interrupt the operation or programming structure of the EzLicence website, or attempt or encourage anyone else to do so.

Warranty

13.   EzLicence agrees that its products come with guarantees under the Australian Consumer Law that cannot be excluded under this Agreement. For more information, please visit https://lawhandbook.sa.gov.au/

14.   EzLicence guarantees that its services to the Learner will be rendered with due care and skill, be fit for purpose, and be supplied within a reasonable time.

15.   The Learner acknowledges and accepts that the services of EzLicence are strictly limited to those set out in clauses 5 - 8 (inclusive).

16.   To the maximum extent permitted by law, EzLicence’s liability to the Learner for a breach of clauses 13 and 14 is limited at the absolute discretion of EzLicence to –

16.1.  the re-supply of its services, or

16.2.  payment of the cost of having those services supplied again.

Fidelity

17.   The Learner agrees not to do any acts or omit to do things that damages or may damage the reputation of EzLicence.

Lessons

18.   The Learner agrees to comply with the following rules, which, include, but are not limited to, –

18.1.  arriving punctually at lessons with Driving Instructors;

18.2.  following all instructions of the Driving Instructor;

18.3.  providing a full and valid pick-up address;

18.4.  providing a mobile phone number that is contactable via sms and calls

18.5.  acting safely, abiding by all driving laws, rules, and regulations in their jurisdiction, being aware of risks, and not putting people or property in danger (including motor vehicles);

18.6. being respectful to Driving Instructors;

18.7.  not using a mobile phone or similar electronic device while undertaking lessons;

18.8.  not offering money, tips, or other types of bribes to Driving Instructors;

18.9.  not encouraging a Driving Instructor, or accepting an offer by a Driving Instructor, to personally undertake lessons with that Driving Instructor outside of, or separate from, the EzLicence platform;

18.10. taking reasonable steps to mitigate loss; and

18.11. indemnifying EzLicence against loss (pursuant to clauses 40 and 41).

Tax Compliance

19.   The Learner agrees to pay any applicable taxes when transacting through the EzLicence website or any associated websites.

Learner Payment Terms

20.   The Learner agrees to the EzLicence Payment Policy, in force from time to time, as currently set out in Schedule 1.

21.   The Learner acknowledges and agrees that –

21.1.  they are immediately liable to pay for bookings and payment will be processed immediately when a booking is made; and

21.2.  when they enter their respective credit or debit card details, they are authorised to use that card for the transaction.

22.   When a Learner pays for a booking or bookings, a credit or credits will be issued to that Learner for the value of each booking paid which will be held on account by EzLicence. Credits issued or arising under this clause 22 or clauses 30 or 33.1 –

22.1.  are non-refundable, non-transferrable, and may not be assigned to or used by any other person other than the Learner to which the credit has been issued;

22.2.  must be used to purchase a booking, or refunded according to clause 26, within 24 months of the date of the Learner making payment, after which time it will expire and be forfeited to EzLicence, who –

22.2.1.  at that time, will have clear and absolute title to those forfeited credits; and

22.2.2.  on request of the Learner, and if the expired credit is for an amount more than $82.50, will issue to the Learner a tax invoice for the amount forfeited to EzLicence; and

22.3.  EzLicence is entitled to use payments for all credits held on account for Learners and Driving Instructors for any purpose and at any time while those credits are being held on account by EzLicence.

23.   EzLicence can refuse a payment if it believes that –

23.1.  the credit or debit card holder did not authorise a transaction;

23.2.  a transaction is considered fraudulent or suspicious; or

23.3.  in any other circumstances as determined at the sole discretion of EzLicence.

24.   Learners agree that Driving Instructors, and not EzLicence, bear all risks associated with any fraudulent or unauthorised payments made by a Learner.

25.   A Learner who signs up for an EzLicence account also agrees to –

25.1.  the current Stripe payment terms and conditions which can be accessed at https://stripe.com/au/legal ; and

25.2.  the current PayPal terms and conditions which can be accessed at https://www.paypal.com/au/webapps/mpp/ua/legalhub-full

Refunds

26.   Refunds for lessons that have been completed and processed will be provided according to the Payment Policy at clauses 15 to 18.

Promotions by third parties

27.   The Learner agrees that EzLicence may issue reward cards, gift cards, vouchers or promotional cards in connection with third party offers or services to Learners from time to time, at the sole discretion of EzLicence.

28.   It is the sole responsibility of the Learner to read and understand the relevant terms and conditions of the provider (which may not be EzLicence) of the promotions or offers referred to in clause 27 above.

Policies

29.   The Learner warrants that they have read and understood the following documents:

29.1.  the EzLicence Payment Policy;

29.2.  the EzLicence Privacy Policy; and

29.3.  any other documents as reasonably required by EzLicence from time to time.

Lesson Cancellation/Rescheduling Fee

30.   If the Learner cancels or reschedules more than 24 hours before a lesson, the Learner will be issued a credit by EzLicence for 100% of the value of the booking fee.

31.   If the Learner cancels or reschedules 24 hours or less before the lesson is due to start, or in a ‘no show’, then –

31.1.  Driving Instructor will be entitled to charge the Learner for the total price of that booking; and

31.2.  Learner acknowledges and agrees that the fee for cancellation or rescheduling referred to in clause 31.1, being the total price of the booking, is not excessive and relates to the reasonable costs and expenses incurred by EzLicence and the Driving Instructor.

Lesson Cancellation/Reschedule by Driving Instructor

32.   If a Driving Instructor cancels a lesson at any time before that lesson,  a Learner may choose to find a replacement Driving Instructor to make an alternative booking.

33.   If, and only if, a replacement Driving Instructor is not available (referred to in clause 32), EzLicence will provide, at the Learner’s option:

33.1.  a credit in accordance with clause 22; or

33.2.  a refund to the cardholder for the cash paid.

Costs

34.   The Learner agrees that EzLicence may alter its booking fees, or other fees as the case may be, from time to time and at its discretion, and –

34.1.  the price per booking fluctuates and is determined by the individual Driving Instructor, and is based on a number of factors as set out in the EzLicence Payment Policy;

34.2.  the Learner will be notified of all booking charges and additional fees at the point of booking and be required to accept such booking charges and additional fees before payment is made;

34.3.  the Learner will be given 7 days’ notice (by means of a website notice or similar notice or announcement) if a substantial increase in booking charges or additional fees; and

34.4.  any changes in the instructor's price between when a booking is made and when the actual lesson takes place will not affect the booking.

34.5.  EzLicence reserves the right to adjust its fees accordingly at its own discretion. This includes discount offers and any booking fees it may wish to charge in the future. 

Events of Termination

35.   The Learner agrees that EzLicence may restrict, suspend or cancel the Learner’s registration on the EzLicence website immediately after providing Written Notice in a number of circumstances which include but are not limited to:

35.1.  where a Learner breaches this Agreement;

35.2. where a learner has an active payment dispute via their payment provider

35.3.  where a Learner has broken the law, including where the Learner has been charged with or has been convicted of a criminal offence;

35.4.  where a Learner is no longer a licensed driver or not otherwise permitted by law to be a licensed driver;

35.5.  where a Learner is the subject of proceedings relating to a motor vehicle accident; and

35.6.  in any other circumstances as determined at the sole discretion of EzLicence.

36.   The Learner agrees that if they fail to perform their obligations under this Agreement and do not rectify the fault within 14 days after being provided notice of such breaches, EzLicence may restrict, suspend or cancel a Learner’s registration on the EzLicence website by giving Written Notice to the Learner.

37.   The Learner agrees that they may terminate this Agreement by de-registering their account on the EzLicence website at any time.

MISCELLANEOUS

Confidentiality

38.   Learners agree that they are not to use Driving Instructor information except for the express purpose of undertaking or organising lessons.

Indemnity

39.   Learners agree to indemnify and protect EzLicence against any actions, damages, claims, or demands including through negligence which occur as a result or in relation to their actions or omissions.

40.   These clauses 38 and 39 survive the termination of this Agreement.

Privacy

41.   EzLicence and Driving Instructors agree to adhere to the Australian Privacy Principles from time to time in force (if applicable) and that the EzLicence Privacy Policy forms part of this Agreement.

42.   Learners agree that EzLicence may collect and use Learner information and personal data for marketing purposes and for the provision of services through or by the EzLicence website.

43.   Learners agree that EzLicence may collect their name, location and address, contact details, age, gender, and financial information.

44.   Learners agree and understand that they may obtain access to personal information that EzLicence has on record by sending a support ticket via https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new. Learners agree and acknowledge that they have read the Privacy Policy and accept its terms including any subsequent additions or variations to this policy which EzLicence may make from time to time.

Intellectual Property

45.   The Learner agrees that all intellectual property and copyright rights over or in connection with EzLicence products and resources are owned by EzLicence exclusively.

Force Majeure

46.   Both parties agree that neither EzLicence nor Learners are liable to the other or considered to be in default under this Agreement if and to the extent that such party’s performance of these Terms and Conditions is delayed or prevented by reason of force majeure, which is defined for this Agreement to mean an event that is beyond the reasonable control of the party affected and occurs without such party’s fault or negligence. In the event of a force majeure event reasonable measures will be taken to accommodate an equivalent booking for a future date where appropriate. 

Arbitration

47.   The parties agree that if any dispute arises between a Learner and EzLicence, the dispute can be managed in ways that include but are not limited to –

47.1.  if the dispute is not resolved within 14 days, the parties agree to refer the matter to a mediator in accordance with the following procedure:

47.2.  to hire a mediator to mediate over the dispute;

47.3.  the mediation can take place via video conference; and

47.4.  the mediator agrees to follow the mediation procedure set out by the Resolution Institute. A copy of the mediation rules may be accessed at this website: https://www.resolution.institute/documents/item/1897.

47.5.  if the mediation process fails, the parties agree to refer the matter to an arbitrator in accordance with the following procedures:

47.6.  the arbitration can be conducted by a single arbitrator agreed by the parties;

47.7.  if the parties are unable to agree on the arbitrator within 14 days, the arbitrator is to be appointed by the President of the Institute of Arbitrators Australia;

47.8.  the arbitration will be conducted in accordance with the Rules of Conduct of Commercial Arbitrators and any guidelines issued by the Institute of Arbitrators;

47.9.  the parties agree that the decision of the arbitrator is binding on the parties; and

47.10.  the parties agree to share the costs of a mediation or arbitration.

No Waiver

48.   The parties agree that a failure or delay by EzLicence in exercising a right does not constitute a waiver of EzLicences rights to pursue an action.

No Assignment of Agreement

49.   The Learner agrees that they are not allowed to sell, transfer, assign, sub-lease or transfer this Agreement to anyone else.

Modification of Agreement

50.   The parties agree that EzLicence may change this Agreement from time to time with notice to the Learner according to clause 51 below, such that:

50.1.  a Learner who continues to use the EzLicence website is considered to have accepted the new terms as updated from time to time; and

50.2.  it is the responsibility of the Learner to read through the amended Terms and Conditions as required from time to time.

Notice

51.   The parties agree that any Notices provided under this Agreement may be provided by:

51.1.  Email or SMS;

51.2.  any other form of electronic communication, including via the EzLicence website; or

51.3.  by any other means used on a regular basis by both parties.

Severability

52.   This Agreement is to be interpreted in a way that renders the provisions valid and enforceable to the maximum extent possible.

Governing Law

53.   This Agreement is governed by the laws of South Australia. The parties submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts in that State.

Entire Agreement

54.   This Agreement represents the entire agreement between the parties, along with any documents referred to herein, and supersedes any previous understandings or agreements, whether oral or written.

Definitions

55.   The following words carry the following meanings: 

Agreement means this document as amended from time to time along with any documents referred to in this document;

Driving Instructor means the person who has contracted with EzLicence to become a Driving Instructor through the EzLicence online website platform;

Event of Termination means those events set out in clauses 35. – 37.

EzLicence means EzLicence Pty Ltd;

Learner means a person who has made a booking through EzLicence;

Learner Permit means the licence that a person who is at least 16 years old who had successfully met all of the requirements to be issued a permit allowing them to drive while being supervised by a qualified supervising driver;

Payment Policy means the EzLicence Payment Policy;

Policies means the Payment Policy and the Privacy Policy.

Privacy Policy means the EzLicence Privacy Policy; and

Written Notice includes notification given by email or SMS.

Interpretation

In the interpretation of this Agreement –

every aspect of this document with the exception of the headings are substantive parts of this Agreement and are to be read accordingly;

headings are used for convenience, but are not to be interpreted as part of this Agreement;

when the singular is mentioned, it also means the plural of that word, and vice versa;

words specifying one gender includes all other genders; and

when legislation is referred to, the legislation includes all rules, ordinances, by laws, orders, regulations, consolidations, rewrites, amendments, re-enactments and replacements of that same legislation in force from time to time.

articles.title: EzLicence Privacy Policy
articles.excerpt: 1.   Our Privacy Policy explains our practices relating to the collection, use and disclosure of information, including personal...
articles.content:
1.   Our Privacy Policy explains our practices relating to the collection, use and disclosure of information, including personal information which we may collect from time to time:

1.1.  We are committed to ensuring that your information is stored safely and responsibly. We are committed to complying with privacy laws which apply to us;

1.2.  The Privacy Act protects personal information and requires that we comply with the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs);

2.   By visiting this website and purchasing products of the Company, you acknowledge and agree to our:

2.1.  Privacy Policy; and

2.2.  Terms and Conditions.
https://www.ezlicence.com.au/terms-and-conditions-instructors

Learner Terms and Conditions

Contacting us

3.   You may contact us at any time for details regarding this policy; and

4.   For any questions concerning your account or your personal information or cookies please send us a support request at https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new. 

Collection of Information

5.   We collect the personal information of Learner Drivers which are provided to us including your:

5.1.  Name;

5.2.  Date of Birth;

5.3.  Pickup address;

5.4.  Billing address (which is stored through Stripe and not through the EzLicence server);

5.5.  Email address;

5.6.  Details of the products you have purchased;

5.7.  Any additional information you provide to use through use of our website;

5.8.  Customer number; and

5.9.  Telephone number;

EzLicence may pass these details on to Driving Instructors for the purpose of delivering driving lessons.

6.   We collect the personal information of Driving Instructors, including your:

6.1.  Name;

6.2.  Date of Birth;

6.3.  Gender;

6.4.  Billing address (which is stored through Stripe and not through the EzLicence server);

6.5.  Email address;

6.6.  Telephone number;

6.7.  Driver's Licence details;

6.8.  Driver Instructor’s Licence details;

6.9.  WWCC numbers; and

6.10.  Vehicle Registration Number;

EzLicence may pass on details to Learner Drivers for the purpose of undertaking driving lessons for information including but not limited to a Driving Instructor's name, email address and telephone number.

7.   This information is collected in a number of ways including through:

7.1.  The account creation process;

7.2.  Online forms;

7.3.  Order Placement;

7.4.  Through your interactions with our customer service team;

7.5.  Comments;

7.6.  Account settings;

7.7.  Preferences;

7.8.  Ratings; and

7.9.  all similar types of data as determined from time to time by EzLicence;

8.   We may also collect data from other sources which may not always be obvious such as through the use of “cookies”;

9.   We may also gather information from both online and offline data providers which could include internet browsing behaviour, demographic data or interest based data;

10.   Where you provide us information about a third party, you warrant that you have the consent of that third party before providing us with that information;

11.   We do not collect and we are not responsible for the collection or security of payment details;

12.   Payment information is processed and stored using a third party company:

12.1.   This company can be contacted at info@stripe.com; and

12.2.  Before entering your personal details we suggest that you read and become familiar with the privacy policy for any third party company.

Why we collect this information

13.   The personal information you provide will be used to ensure Products are delivered to you and to provide offers, information and our Products to you. We also use this information to improve and enhance our services and marketing efforts;

14.   You have the right to inspect your personal information:

14.1.  You always have the right to request that we delete or correct the information held about you; and

14.2.  If you wish to alter, access or remove or complain about breaches of the APP please send us a support request at https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new;

15.    When you browse our website we also automatically receive your computer’s internet protocol (IP) address in order to provide us with information that helps us learn about your browser and operating system. We may also collect data regarding:

15.1.  Device IDs or identifiers;

15.2.  Connection information;

15.3.  Device software;

15.4   Statistics of page views;

15.5.  Advertisement data;

15.6.  Referral URLs; and

15.7.  IP data;

16.   We use this data to determine your general geographic location so that we may provide location specific content to you;

17.   We may use your information to assist in responding to complaints and enquiries;

18.   We may also investigate any possible breach of our Conditions including fraud or illegal activities, with the use of your personal information;

19.   Email marketing (if applicable):

19.1.  With your permission, we may send you emails about:

19.1.1.  our website;

19.1.2.  new products; and

19.1.3.  other updates;

19.2.  If we ask for your personal information for a secondary purpose such as marketing, we will either ask you directly for your expressed consent or provide you with an opportunity to say no; and

19.3.  If after you opt-in you change your mind you may withdraw your consent for us to contact you for the continued collection, use or disclosure of your information, at anytime by sending us a support request at https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new

20.  Your personal information will not be shared, sold, rented or disclosed other than as described in this Privacy Policy.

Disclosure

21.   We may collect, hold, use and disclose your information for purposes which are within reasonable expectations or where permitted by law, enforce applicable terms of use or protect against infringements to the rights, property of EzLicence, our other Customers and the public;

22.  We may share your information with other companies, employees or independent contractors of EzLicence including for:

22.1.  Customer support;

22.2.  Service improvement;

22.3.  Development;

22.4.  Processing;

22.5.  Storage; or

22.6.  Providing access to our Products, including by providing this information to Driving Instructors for the purpose of providing driving lessons to Learner Drivers (or visa versa) in particular providing information regarding:

22.6.1.  Names;

22.6.2.  Phone numbers;

22.6.3.  Email addresses;

22.6.4.  Learner Driver pick up addresses; and

22.6.5.  all other similar information required in the provision of driving lessons.

23.   We may disclose feedback from Learner Drivers to Driving Instructors, or visa versa;

24.  We may disclose personal information collected for purposes which are incidental to the sale and promotion of our Products to you;

25.  We use other companies, contractors or agents to provide services to you including:

25.1.  Delivery;

25.2.  Marketing;

25.3.  Communications;

25.4.  Legal services;

25.5.  Debt collection;

25.6.  Administration services;

25.7.  Customer services;

25.8.  Information technology providers;

25.9.  Credit card; or

25.10  Other Payment Methods to conduct transactions;

The third parties to whom we disclose personal information may be located within or outside Australia.

Data Storage

26.   Your data is stored through the www.ezlicence.com.au data storage and databases; and

27.   Stripe stores any information relating to payments.

Payment

28.   If you choose a direct payment gateway to complete your purchase, www.stripe.com/au stores your credit card data;

28.1.  It is encrypted through the Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI-DSS);

28.2   Your purchase transaction data is stored only as long as is necessary to complete your purchase transaction; and

28.3.  After that is complete, your purchase transaction information is deleted;

29.   All direct payment gateways adhere to the standards set by PCI-DSS as managed by the PCI Security Standards Council, which is a joint effort of brands like Visa, MasterCard, American Express and Discover.

Third-Party Services

30.  In general, the third-party providers used by us will only collect, use and disclose your information to the extent necessary to allow them to perform the services they provide to us;

31.  However, certain third-party service providers such as payment gateways and other payment transaction processors have their own privacy policies in respect to the information we are required to provide to them for your purchase-related transactions;

32.  For these providers we recommend that you read their privacy policies so you can understand the manner in which your personal information will be handled by these providers;

33.  Certain providers may be located in or have facilities that are located outside of Australia on foreign servers. If you elect to proceed with a transaction that involves the services of a third-party service provider then your information may become subject to the laws of the jurisdiction(s) in which that service provider or its facilities are located;

34.  As an example, if you are located in Canada and your transaction is processed by a payment gateway located in the United States, then your personal information used in completing that transaction may be subject to disclosure under United States legislation, including the Patriot Act;

35.  Once you leave our website or are redirected to a third-party website or application, you are no longer governed by this Privacy Policy or our website’s Terms of Service.

Third Party Websites

36.  When you click on links on our website, they may direct you away from our site;

37.  We are not responsible for the privacy practices of other sites and encourage you to read their privacy statements and terms and conditions prior to finalising any transaction.

Security

38.  We take reasonable precautions to protect personal information and follow industry best practices to make sure it is not:

38.1.  Inappropriately lost;

38.2.  Misused;

38.3.  Accessed;

38.4.  Disclosed;

38.5.  Altered; or

38.6.  Destroyed;

39.  If you provide us with your credit card information, the information is encrypted using secure socket layer technology (SSL) and stored with a AES-256 encryption;

40.  Although no method of transmission over the Internet or electronic storage is 100% secure, we follow all PCI-DSS requirements and implement additional generally accepted industry standards;

41.  We will use our best endeavours to ensure the safety of your personal information. However, we cannot guarantee that our systems will not be compromised or interfered with by any third party. You acknowledge that by providing us with your personal information you do so at your own risk.

Cookies

42.   EzLicence uses cookies in accordance with the Electronic Communications Act 1999 (Cth);

42.1.  A cookie is a small text file stored on your computer that contains information that helps the website to identify and track the visitor;

42.2.  Cookies do no harm to your computer, consist only of text, cannot contain viruses and occupies virtually no space on your hard drive;

42.3.  There are two types of cookies: "Session Cookies" and cookies that are saved permanently on your computer;

43.   The first type of cookie commonly used is "Session Cookies". During the time you visit the website, our web server assigns your browser a unique identifier string so as not to confuse you with other visitors.;

43.1.  A "Session Cookie" is never stored permanently on your computer and disappears when you close your browser;

43.2.  To use www.ezlicence.com.au without difficulty you need to have cookies enabled;

44.   The second type of cookie saves a file permanently on your computer;

44.1.  This type of cookie is used to track how visitors move around on the website;

44.2.  This is only used to offer visitors better services and support and the text files can be deleted;

44.3.  On this website we use this type of cookie to keep track of your shopping cart and to keep statistics of our visitors; and

44.4.  The information stored on your computer is only a unique number, without any connection to personal information;

45.   To opt-out of cookies, you can alter the settings on your internet browser to accept or reject a website from using cookies. This may affect functionality of the website; and

46.   Some third parties may use cookies and other technologies such as Facebook pixels. We recommend that you read their privacy policies or policies relating to the use of cookies and these other technologies.

Marketing

47.  We have implemented the following marketing tools;

47.1.  Remarketing;

47.2.  Google display;

47.3.  Network impression reporting;

47.4.  Doubleclick campaign manager integration; and

47.5   Google analytics demographic and interests reporting;

48.   Using the Ads Settings in google you many opt out of google analytics for display advertising and customize google display network ads;

49.  We reserve the right to use remarketing with google analytics to advertise online;

49.1.  Third-party vendors, including Google, may show you our ads on sites across the internet;

49.2.  EzLicence and third-party vendors, including Google use first-party cookies (such as the Google Analytics cookie) and third-party cookies (such as the DoubleClick cookie) together to inform, optimize, and serve ads based on users past visits to our website;

49.3.  EzLicence and third-party vendors, including Google, use first-party cookies (such as the Google Analytics cookies) and third-party cookies (such as the DoubleClick cookie) together to report how ad impressions, other uses of ad services, and interactions with these ad impressions and ad services are related to visits to this site; and

49.4.  EzLicence collects data from Google's Interest-based advertising or 3rd-party audience data (such as age, gender, and interests) with Google Analytics.

Changes To This Privacy Policy

50.   We reserve the right to modify this privacy policy at any time so please review it frequently;

50.1.  Changes and clarifications will take effect immediately upon their posting on the website;

50.2.  If we make material changes to this policy, we will notify you here that it has been updated so that you are aware of:

50.2.1.  What information we collect;

50.2.2.  How we use it; and

50.2.3.  Under what circumstances, if any, we use and/or disclose it;

51.  If our website is acquired or merged with another company, your information may be transferred to the new owners so that we may continue to sell products to you.

Questions and Contact Information

52.   If you would like to access, correct, amend or delete any personal information we have about you, register a complaint, or simply want more information please send us a support request at https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new.

articles.excerpt: SCHEDULE 1 - EzLicence Payment Policy All terms defined in the Driving Instructor Terms and Conditions and the Learner Terms and...
articles.content:
SCHEDULE 1 - EzLicence Payment Policy

All terms defined in the Driving Instructor Terms and Conditions and the Learner Terms and Conditions have the same meaning as set out there and are incorporated into this Payment Policy. 

Parties

This EzLicence Payment Policy is binding on each Driving Instructor and Learner, and is a supplementary schedule to the Driving Instructor Terms and Conditions and the Learner Terms and Conditions respectively.

Booking Prices

1.   The price for a booking is set by the Driving Instructor and may be based on a number of factors including, but not limited to, pick-up location of the Learner, the vehicle transmission, and the particular Driving Instructor selected.

2.  All booking prices Discount Bands referred to in this Agreement are based on one hour bookings.

3.  A Learner will be notified of the total price per booking, and must confirm the total price for each transaction before payment will be made to the Driving Instructor.

Discount Bands

4.   If a Learner makes payment for the equivalent of multiple booking hours in the same transaction, they may receive a discount according to the following Discount Bands, whereby the discounts are based on, and calculated, from the booking price referred to in clause 1 -

4.1.   Equivalent to 0 - 5 bookings hours' worth of credits = 0% discount;

4.2.  Equivalent to 6 – 9 booking hours' worth of credits = 5% discount per booking hour credit,

4.3.  Equivalent to 10+ booking hours' worth of credits = 10% discount per booking hour credit.

5.   Discount Bands will apply to the price per one hour booking as allocated at the time of purchase. This price is subject to change on subsequent bookings in accordance with clause 1.

6.   Discount Bands are awarded as non-refundable bonus credit, added to a Learner's EzLicence wallet.

7.   The bonus credit will be consumed at the time of booking in proportion to the discount received. For example, if a Learner -

7.1.  Pays for the equivalent of 10 booking hours at $100 per hour in one transaction and receives a 10% discount in the form of bonus credit. Pays $900 and receives $100 bonus credit; and

7.2.  Books a lesson at $100 per hour, $10 of the bonus credit (10%) will be consumed.

8.   The Discount Bands will be applied if a Learner pays for 6 or more booking hours in one transaction, and is not cumulative across multiple transactions. For example, if a Learner -

8.1.  pays for the equivalent of 8 booking hours in one transaction, all of those booking hours will be at a 5% discount; and

8.2.  has already paid for 8 booking hours, and in their next transaction pays for the equivalent of 8 booking hours, a 10% discount will not apply to those additional bookings.

9.    Discount Bands apply irrespective of whether a Learner changes Driving Instructors.

10.  If a Learner is granted a refund, the refunded amount will be based on consumption of credit rather than the purchase value. For example, if a Learner -

10.1.  Pays for the equivalent of 10 booking hours at $100 per hour in one transaction and receives a 10% discount in the form of bonus credit. Pays $900 and receives $100 bonus credit; and, all of those booking hours will be at a 5% discount; and

10.2.  Completes 5 booking hours at $100 before being granted a refund for their remaining balance. The learner will only receive a refund of $450, as they are not entitled to the $50 of bonus credit remaining.

11.  From time to time, EzLicence may offer other offers, including discounted booking packages, of which the Discount Bands may not apply.

Platform Processing Fees

12.   This fee supports our ongoing efforts to maintain the highest standards of safety and quality, including verification of instructor qualifications and Working With Children Checks.

13.   The fee is based on the credit amount you purchase (excluding any bonus credit).

14.   The fee is non-refundable.

15.   Any changes to the Platform Processing Fee will be communicated through our website or email.

Cancellations and rescheduling

16.   Any cancellations made by a learner within 24 hours of a booking start time is considered a late cancellation. 

17.  Under the Booking Modification Policy, the Driving Instructor may do either of the following -

17.1.  require that the Learner pay the total cost of the booking; or

17.2.  reverse the payment entirely and allow the Learner to reschedule their booking with no charge.

18.  In the event of a late cancellation, the Driving Instructor will be paid the Late Cancellation Fee. The Driving Instructor has 30 days to apply clause 10.2. If no option is selected within this time frame, then clause 10.1 will automatically apply.

Lesson payment validation

19.  Driving Instructor payment is automatically applied at the end time of each booking. EzLicence assumes that the booking was completed.

20.  If a completed booking is reversed by the Driving Instructor or EzLicence within 30 days of booking end time, the full booking payment will remain as a credit for the Learner to use for subsequent bookings, and clauses 22 and 26 of the Learner Terms and Conditions will apply.

21.   The reversed booking payment will be subtracted from the instructor's current or subsequent payout.

Refunds for validated bookings

22.  From time to time, EzLicence may receive requests to refund payments made to a Driving Instructor for completed bookings.

23.  Any payment disputes raise via a payment provider after service delivery will result in debt collection and may be subject to additional fees.

24.  EzLicence will investigate all refund requests and, at its sole discretion, make a determination whether a refund should be paid.

25.  At its sole discretion, and subject to the Learner Terms and Conditions and the Driving Instructor Terms and Conditions, EzLicence may provide a full refund on one of the following situations -

25.1.  it is brought to EzLicence’s attention that the payment received was due to fraudulent activity or was otherwise not authorized by the cardholder;

25.2.  the Driving Instructor failed to reverse a booking payment for a booking the Learner did not take due to a fault on the part of the Driving Instructor; or

25.3.  a complaint is received and it is determined that the Driving Instructor breached the Driving Instructor Terms and Conditions.

articles.content:
EzLicence Refer a Friend or Family Reward Program Terms and Conditions.

As a Refer A Friend or Family Reward Program member (a “Referring Learner”), you are subject to EzLicence’s Learner Driver Terms and Conditions, and EzLicence’s Privacy Policy (both available on our website), as well as the following additional Terms & Conditions for EzLicence’s Refer A Friend or Family Reward Program:

1. Qualified Referral. A Qualified Referral is defined as a purchase made at www.ezlicence.com.au by a learner (a "Referred Learner") who arrives at our website by clicking your Referral Program link or entering your referral code during their signup. You are limited to one Qualified Referral for each Referred Learner; in other words, additional/repeat purchases made by a Referred Learner are not counted as additional Qualified Referrals. There's no minimum purchase required for Qualified Referral.

2. Referred Learner. The Referred Learner and the Referring Learner cannot be the same person (for example, by using a different email address). For additional clarity, the learner receiving the driving lesson under the Referred Learner’s account, cannot be the same person who was receiving the driving lesson under the Referring Learner’s account. The learner has to be a new learner who has never made a booking with an Driving Instructor on EzLicence. We automatically disqualify suspicious referrals. If someone you referred made their first purchase using your EzLicence referral link and you didn’t receive your reward, your referral might have been flagged as suspicious. Please send us a support ticket at https://support.ezlicence.com.au/hc/en-au/requests/new, and we'll investigate this for you.

3.  Referral Rewards. For you to earn referral rewards as a Referring Learner, both you and the Referred Learner must have purchased and completed  at least one booking on EzLicence. If either the Referring Learner or the Referred Learner initiates a refund, a payment dispute or the payment is believed to be fraudulent, EzLicence will remove any reward credits applied to both the Referred Learner and the Referring Learner’s EzLicence accounts. If reward credits have already been paid out, we will seek reimbursement for any expense incurred by EzLicence.

4.  As a Referring Learner, you are responsible for any and all tax liability resulting from Referral Rewards.

5.  Eligibility. Eligibility is limited to individuals only. EzLicence’’s Refer A Friend or Family Reward Program cannot be used by businesses for affiliate lead generation as determined in EzLicence’s sole discretion.

6.  Expiry date. Referral rewards are valid for 36 months from the time of issue. You must cash out your reward balance to a PayPal account within this time period, otherwise the referral rewards will expire.

7.  No Spam. You must comply with all up-to-date “SPAM” laws. For example, emails must be created and distributed in a personal manner, and bulk email distribution is strongly discouraged. Any distribution of your referral link that could constitute unsolicited commercial email or "spam" under any applicable law or regulation is expressly prohibited and will be grounds for immediate termination of your account and exclusion from EzLicence's Refer A Friend Program.

8.  Right to Close Accounts. EzLicence reserves the right to close the account(s) of any Referring Learner and/or Referred Learner and to request proper payment if the Referring Learner and/or Referred Learner attempts to use the EzLicence Refer A Friend Program in a questionable manner or breaches any of these Terms & Conditions or is in violation of any law, statute or governmental regulation.

9.  Right to Cancel Program or Change Terms. EzLicence reserves the right to cancel our Refer A Friend or Family Reward Program or to change these Terms & Conditions at any time in its sole discretion. Any unclaimed referral rewards will be forfeited at that time.

articles.title: Just a moment...
Refund Policy
articles.excerpt: We hope that every booking you place via the EzLicence platform goes ahead without issue but we understand that sometimes things happen....
articles.content:
We hope that every booking you place via the EzLicence platform goes ahead without issue but we understand that sometimes things happen. If a booking does not meet your expectations, and you would like a refund, please submit a request to our Support Team. We do our best to facilitate fair refunds to Learners, to see if you are eligible for a refund, please read below:

Cooling-off period

We offer a 48 hour cooling-off period. If you make a purchase, don’t book in any services and decide you're not quite ready to commit, just submit a refund request within 48 hours of your original transaction we will provide you with a ‘no questions asked’ refund.

When am I eligible to receive a refund?

Examples of when you may be eligible to receive a refund include:

  • There are no available driving instructors servicing in your location
  • There are no driving instructors available within the next 4 weeks
  • You have accidentally processed your payment multiple times
  • You have completed over 10 hours of bookings and have lesson credits left over that you no longer need
  • Your driving instructor has not arrived for your booking
  • Your instructor has cancelled your booking 

Refund limitations for Afterpay and PayPal purchases

While we aim to process all eligible refunds under the EzLicence policy, please note that some payment providers have restrictions that affect our ability to do so. Specifically:

  • Afterpay transactions older than 120 days are not eligible for refunds.
  • PayPal transactions older than 180 days are not eligible for refunds.

These limitations are set by the payment providers and are outside of EzLicence's control. If your purchase falls outside these timeframes, we are unfortunately unable to process your refund, even if you would otherwise qualify under our refund policy.

When am I not eligible to receive a refund?

Examples of when you will not be eligible to receive a refund include, but are not limited to:

  • Your wallet value is zero or has expired
  • A change of mind, including:
    • Found the service cheaper
    • Have completed your logbook hours and no longer need the service
    • No longer wish to use the service
    • You have relocated since your original purchase
  • Your preferred instructor is not available, however other instructors are available
  • An issue with the driving instructor not meeting your quality expectations
    • Tip: You can provide an instructor rating via your learner account
    • Tip: You can change instructor online at any time
    • You can discuss this directly with your instructor to request a payment reversal
  • A booking issue that was caused by user error including:
    • Not holding the correct licence
    • Booking the wrong service
    • Providing an incorrect pick up address
    • Providing incorrect contact telephone number
    • Not meeting the licencing requirements to sit your driving test
    • Failing to book your driving test directly with the test centre
  • A booking issue caused by an external source including:
    • Local licensing authority
  • You have requested a late cancellation and have been charged by your instructor
  • You have an active payment dispute
  • Platform processing fees are non-refundable

Gift Vouchers

All EzLicence gift vouchers are valid for 36 months from the date of purchase. A refund will be provided for any valid and unredeemed gift vouchers upon request. A voucher is considered redeemed once it has been added to a learner account, either by creating a new account or applying it to an existing one. Once redeemed, vouchers are no longer eligible for a refund. In the case of a refund, the amount will be returned to the original purchaser, not the recipient.

Resolving Your Request

You can request a refund or a general complaint by submitting a support request. We encourage you to provide as much information as possible to speed up the process. If we determine you are eligible, you should expect any refunds to be returned to your original payment method within 5-10 business days, depending on your bank.

Platform Processing Fees

Please be aware that platform processing fees are non-refundable. These fees cover the costs associated with maintaining a high standard of safety, technology, and service across the EzLicence platform. As a result, any approved refund will be for the booking amount excluding the processing fee, meaning the total refunded may be less than your original payment.

articles.title: How to reschedule a booking from your calendar
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.You are trying to: Reschedule an existing...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Reschedule an existing booking in your calendar.

Overview:

EzLicence provides you with maximum control and flexibility over your calendar. The platform assists you to streamline your booking management, allowing you to effortlessly keep your calendar up to date and communicate with your learners for an all-round smooth booking experience. 

Below are our top tips to ensure the best outcome for you and your learner when you need to reschedule a booking:

  1. The more notice the better - learners will be more flexible if notified in advance of an issue with their booking.
  2. Make contact with the learner - it is always best to discuss rescheduling directly with your learner so you can make the new booking at a mutually convenient time. This also gives you the opportunity to let the learner know the legitimate reason for rescheduling, which can make for a much better experience.
  3. Minimise the impact - rescheduling on the same learner multiple times can be a point of frustration, choose wisely.
  4. Avoid a learner’s first booking - a learner’s first driving lesson is a big milestone! Learning to drive is both exciting and daunting, rescheduling a learners first lesson before you have established a relationship with them can impact their decision to continue with you or change instructors.

You can reschedule a booking from a number of places in your EzLicence account - upcoming bookings, learner menu or your calendar.  The process is the same from each place. 

You can reschedule a booking any time before a booking start time. 

Let’s take a closer look. 

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to reschedule a booking from your calendar. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to reschedule a booking from your calendar

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

Step 3.

Locate and click on the booking you wish to reschedule

Step 4.

From the Booking details, click Modify Booking a drop down menu will appear

Step 5. 

From the drop down menu, select Reschedule booking

Step 6.

The Reschedule Booking screen will appear, there is a x Discard Changes button at the top if required. Below this the current booking crossed out and marked as cancelled, this action will only occur once this process is completed.

Step 7.

Scroll down to view the new booking information, select the new date and time from the drop down menu. If required, you can also change the pickup location.

Step 8.

Click the Propose Booking button.

Step 9.

The original booking will now be cancelled and removed from your calendar. The new proposed booking will be visible.

Important

Using the reschedule feature will remove the original booking from your calendar and a proposed booking will be sent to your learner via email and SMS. The booking proposal will expire at the booking start time OR 24 hours after it was sent, whichever comes first. For further information on proposed bookings, click here.


If you would prefer to cancel the booking without proposing a new booking, please refer to our support article - How to cancel a booking from your calendar

articles.title: How to cancel a booking from your calendar
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Cancel and remove an...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Cancel and remove an existing booking from your calendar.

Overview:

Unfortunately, there are times when you need to cancel a booking and in some instances, you are unsure of your future availability and are not able to reschedule instead of cancelling.  Whilst we don't encourage you to cancel your learner's bookings, there are some situations which can't be avoided, some examples are:

  • You are unwell and are unsure when you will return to work
  • There is a family emergency
  • Your car has been damaged or has unexpected mechanical issues

The #1 cause of poor instructor reviews is the handling of cancellations. Before cancelling a booking, we strongly recommend you make contact with your learner to discuss it with them and reschedule the lesson instead of cancelling.

There are a number of places you can cancel a booking - upcoming bookings, Learner menu or your calendar, the process is the same from each place. You can cancel a booking any time before a booking start time. 

Please note that all cancellations will contribute to the cancellation rate displayed on your dashboard. For further information, please refer to our support article - How your cancellation rate is calculated.

If you would prefer to reschedule the booking, click here.

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to cancel a booking from your calendar. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to cancel a booking from your calendar

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

Step 3.

Locate and click on the booking you wish to cancel.

Step 4.

From the Booking details, click Modify Booking a drop down menu will appear

Step 5. 

From the drop down menu, select Cancel without new booking.

Step 6.

A Cancel Booking notification will appear. Using the dropdown, select a Reason for cancellation.

Step 7.

In the text box below, type a Message for Learner. Take a moment to re-read your message as this cannot be undone.

Step 8.

Check the box next to "I understand and agree to the Cancellation Policy"

Step 9.

Click Cancel Booking. This action cannot be reversed. You have now removed the booking from your calendar and your learner has received a notification.

You have now removed the booking from your calendar and your learner has received a notification. Please ensure that your availability has been updated, we have further information available in our support article - how to temporarily block your calendar.

Important

Cancelling the booking will immediately return a booking credit to the learner account, ready to rebook.

There are some situations where the learner needs their booking as soon as possible and you may not be available. If needed, please refer your learner to the step-by-step instructions - How to change your driving instructor.

Booking time has already passed? Please refer to our support article -How to reverse a payment.
