articles.title: How to temporarily block your calendar
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.You are trying to: Temporarily block your...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Temporarily block your calendar to new bookings.

Overview:

We all know that life happens! There are times when you are unavailable and need to block your calendar for an extended period of time. Some examples are:

  • You are unwell or recovering from illness
  • Your car has been damaged and is out of action
  • You don’t want to work on Public Holidays
  • You are going on a holiday (allow extra time in the event of transit delays or jet lag)
  • You work a rotating roster and are waiting for it to be issued

This is an important step in keeping your calendar up to date and ensuring a great learner experience. It is really quick and easy to do.

Important

Blocking your calendar will mean that learners are unable to book with you, it will not reschedule or cancel any existing bookings. If there are existing bookings you need to move, please refer to our support articles:

  • How to reschedule a booking from your calendar
  • How to cancel a booking

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to temporarily block your calendar. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to temporarily block your calendar

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

Step 3.

Click anywhere on the calendar to Create an Event OR in the top right, click Create Event

Step 4.

Click Create Time Off event. 

Step 5. 

Enter an Event Name (eg. Holiday / Vacation, Sick Leave, Family Leave, Vehicle Out of Service, Professional Development or Custom Time Off). 

Step 6.

Adjust the dates and times for the period of time you are unavailable

Step 7.

Adjust the Travel Buffer Time if you need to allow more travel time in your calendar, default is set to No

Step 8.

Click Save Changes.

You will now have a Pink Time Off event in your calendar preventing you from receiving any new bookings.

Overview:

Keeping your calendar up to date will set you on the right path to delivering a great learner experience. The EzLicence calendar offers a great deal of flexibility whilst still being super easy to use. It is important to understand some of the basics before getting into the nitty gritty. 

How to navigate your calendar

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

                

Step 3.

In the top right, you can change the view by clicking on any of the Day | Week | Month buttons
 

Step 4.

In the top left, you can move day to day or week to week or month to month using the < > buttons
 

Step 5.

In the top left, there is also a Today button which will bring the view back to today.

Step 6.

On the left hand side, you can view the time of day in 15 minute increments.
 

Step 7.

To turn Smart Scheduling View off and on go to Calendar Settings.  For further information on Smart Scheduling click here.

               

Step 8.

There are 9 colours in use in the calendar view, they are as follows:

Grey with diagonal lines = closed, no learners can book directly into your calendar

White = open, but not a booking time shown to learners

Green = booking times available for learners to book

Yellow = driving lesson booking

Light blue = invited learner booking

Light yellow with a darker outline = proposed booking

Dark grey = personal event

Pink = Time Off 

Purple = extra open hours

Red line = the current time

 

Step 9.

You can click on any of the existing calendar bookings to view the details and make changes.
 

Step 10.

Click anywhere on the calendar to Create an Event OR in the top right, click Create Event.

You will then get the option to choose the type of event. 

.

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand the calendar functionality in order to maximise success. 

Overview:

As a driving instructor, your success relies on the professionalism, reliability, and steady growth of your business. The EzLicence booking system enables you to connect with learners in real-time. However, for this to work seamlessly, it's important to maintain accurate availability and bookings in your EzLicence calendar. 

4 keys to successfully managing your calendar and booking availability:

1.   Planning Ahead

Learners can view and book lessons up to 75 days or 90 days in advance via the Calendar Settings. By offering a wide booking window, you increase your chances of filling your calendar with students who plan ahead. Be sure to have future availability set in your calendar.

2.   Public Holidays and Your Calendar

Your calendar is automatically set to display booking slots on public holidays. This means you will continue to accept bookings on those special days. Consider offering available lesson slots on public holidays for learners who have free time on those days or ensure that you have removed your availability if you plan to take the day off.

3.   Last-Minute Bookings: Stay Flexible

Our system allows learners to book lessons based on Instructor's set notice period (default is up to 5 hours) before the desired start time, subject to availability. To cater to last-minute bookings, consider keeping some time slots open for spontaneous learners.

How Notice Periods Work
To make scheduling fair for both instructors and learners, EzLicence applies minimum notice periods and daily cutoffs.
Minimum Notice Period
Instructors choose how much advance notice is required before a booking (e.g. 3 hours, 5 hours, 12 hours, 1 day, or 2 days).
Morning Cutoff (12am–7am)
Bookings made between midnight and 7am will only show availability starting from 7am + the chosen notice period (same day).
Daytime Window (7am–7pm)
During the day, the instructor’s chosen notice period applies as is.
Example: If the notice period is 5 hours and you try to book at 10am, the earliest available time will be 3pm.
Evening Cutoff (7pm–11:59pm)
Bookings made after 7pm will only show availability starting from 7am + the chosen notice period the next day.

Why do we have cutoffs?
These cutoffs ensure instructors have enough time to plan their day, especially for late-night or very early morning bookings.

Your calendar is a valuable asset that can help you attract and retain learners. By following these guidelines and keeping your availability up to date, you're ensuring a smooth booking experience for your students. A well-maintained calendar is a testament to your professionalism and commitment to your learners' needs.

articles.title: How to propose a booking to your learner
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Send a booking to your...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Send a booking to your learner.

Overview:

Generate more repeat bookings! With our user-friendly booking proposals, you can effortlessly send booking proposals to your learners at a time that suits both your schedules.  

  • Propose booking times that align perfectly with your schedule. Aim for those efficient 2-hour bookings!
  • Plan consecutive bookings for learners in the same area to cut down on travel time. 
  • If you're operating two vehicles, propose bookings for learners with the same transmission on the same day. No more vehicle switching headaches!
  • Extend your booking slots by suggesting bookings during your typically off-hours. Got a learner with an early morning test? You can easily accommodate them by sending a booking proposal!

Ready to unlock a more efficient calendar? Let's dive into the details together. 🚗💨

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to propose a booking to your learner. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to propose a booking

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

Step 3.

Click anywhere on the calendar to Create an Event OR in the top right, click Create Event

Step 4.

In this example we are proposing a booking to an EzLicence Learner. Click 'New Booking'.

Step 5.

Click Select a learner. A list of all learners who have completed a booking with you in the past 6 months or have an upcoming booking will be displayed.

Step 6.

Click a booking type, the options are a 1-hour driving lesson, 2-hour driving lesson or a driving test package.

Step 7.

Click and select a day and select a time from the drop down menu.

Step 8. (optional)

You are able to propose more than one booking at a time, scroll down and click + Add Another Booking button. Repeat from step 6 above on this new proposal. 

Step 9.

Once complete, click Propose Booking. Your proposed booking will now appear in your calendar as a light grey event.

Step 10.

From your calendar, you can click on the proposed booking to view the details and/or cancel it. 

Step 11.

You can also view and manage your Pending Proposals from your Dashboard.

What’s Next?

The learner will receive a notification, which will be sent via SMS and email. Learners can accept or reject the proposal from their learner account.

Booking proposals will expire at the booking start time OR after 24 hrs from when it was sent (whichever comes first).

Once it is accepted by the learner, it will change to a yellow confirmed booking.

articles.title: How to change your availability
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.You are trying to: Set your...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Set your availability.

Overview:

The EzLicence calendar enables you to easily and in real time customise the days and times that you are available, as well as block out times when you are not available. In this support article, we will provide you with a step-by-step guide on how to set your operating hours.

When setting your operating hours, it is important to not only consider your needs but also the needs of learners. We recommend ensuring that you offer as much availability as you can in order to attract the maximum number of bookings.

To further assist you, our data suggests the busiest times for driving lessons are as follows:

  1. Evenings and Weekends: Many driving instructors find that evenings (after school or work) and weekends (Saturday and Sunday) are the busiest times. This is when most students have free time to schedule driving lessons without conflicting with their regular commitments.
  2. Afternoons: Afternoons, especially during the weekends, tend to be quite busy as students often choose this time for lessons after school/uni/sport.
  3. During School Breaks: School holidays and breaks can also be peak times for driving instructors, as students have more flexibility in their schedules.

We prioritise your flexibility - you can make changes to your availability at any time, it is easy!

How to set your operating hours

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, on the left side of your screen, click the Settings icon.

Step 3.

Select Opening Hours

 

Step 4. 

You wil see each day of the week listed. End of each day there are there are 3 icons

Close Icon X : use this to close the day of the week when you are unavailable

Add Icon ➕ : use this to add availability in the same day. Scenarios may include where if you wish to offer a split shift, you can simply click this icon. This is a great way to add in a lunch break, maybe you have children you pick up after school or a regular gym class you like to attend.

Copy Icon : use this to copy paste a certain availability block to all the days of the week

 

Step 7. 

Adjust the times as you need

Step 8. 

At the bottom of the page, click the Save Changes button.

 

Important

Making changes to your availability will not change any bookings or events that are already in your calendar.

Your travel time also impacts your overall availability. For further information on how to change your travel time, click here.

If you work in another job that operates on a roster, we recommend that you set your availability and then use your calendar to block out days until your roster is available. For further information on how to use your calendar, click here.

If you want to make yourself available outside of your regular schedule, without permanently changing your weekly hours, you can open up extra availability using extra open hours. This option is great for those one-off times when you’d like to take additional bookings or fit in a learner who can’t make your usual availability. For further information on how to add extra open hours, click here.

This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Change your travel time.

Overview:

Travel time plays an important role in your overall availability. The shorter the allowance for travel time, the more lessons you will be able to deliver. As a result, you will increase your overall profitability.

Another key consideration when selecting your travel time is to ensure you will be on time. Punctuality is essential for building trust and reliability, it demonstrates respect for others' schedules and ensures you have a smooth daily schedule. 

It is all about finding the right balance of both.

How to set your travel time

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, top left hand side of your screen, click the settings icon

Step 3.

Select Calendar Settings

Step 4.

Select travel buffer

Step 5.

From the dropdown select the travel time between lessons.

Step 6. (optional)

If you are operating two vehicles, you will have an additional option, Different Transmissions which is the default travel time between consecutive lessons of different transmission types. Again, select the travel time you want between lessons, where a vehicle changeover is required.

Step 7.

At the bottom of the page, click the Save Changes button.

Important

Making changes to your travel time will not change any bookings or events that are already in your calendar.

We recommend that you allow sufficient time to travel across your service area with an allowance for traffic and congestion. For further information on how to change your service area, click here.

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand how EzLicence works

Overview:

EzLicence is the preferred platform of over 950 independent instructors, and we know that EzLicence will benefit your business too! What makes EzLicence so great? 

  1. Reach more learner drivers - we provide a platform for driving instructors to showcase their services to a wider audience. EzLicence can increase your visibility and attract potential clients to your business.
  2. Convenient scheduling - we offer a user-friendly, real-time scheduling system, allowing instructors to manage their availability and schedule lessons with ease, benefiting both instructors and learners.
  3. Expert marketing support - we assist driving instructors with marketing by featuring their profiles on the platform, making it easier for learners to find and connect with them.
  4. Streamlined payment processing - we streamline the payment process, making it simple for instructors to receive payments from learners and enhancing the financial efficiency of your business.
  5. Convenient online booking - we have an efficient online booking system, both instructors and learners can book and manage driving lessons seamlessly. Save time and reduce every day administrative hassles.
  6. Flexibility - instructors have the flexibility to set their own schedules and hourly rates, giving them more control over their business operations.
  7. Safety and security - we emphasise safety by ensuring that all instructors meet necessary qualifications and certifications. This commitment to safety builds trust with learners.
  8. Trustworthy reviews and ratings - learners are able to leave reviews and ratings, providing valuable feedback for instructors and helping them build a positive online reputation.
  9. Helpful customer support - EzLicence offers customer support to both instructors and learners, addressing any issues or concerns promptly and ensuring a smooth experience for all parties.
  10. Access to resources - instructors can access a variety of resources, including support articles and materials, to enhance their teaching methods and stay updated on industry trends.
  11. Community connection - instructors become part of a community of professionals, fostering networking opportunities and collaboration within the driving education industry.

All of these features and benefits allow driving instructors to streamline their business operations, attract more clients, and provide an enhanced learning experience for their customers.

articles.title: How to make a new booking with your instructor
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are an existing Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Make another booking...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are an existing Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Make another booking with your instructor.

Overview:

Completed your first lesson and eager to book more? You’re in the right spot! 

While you’re planning your learning journey, it’s worth considering booking multiple lessons in advance. Plus, if you purchase a few lessons at a time, you may qualify for a discount! For more detail on purchasing additional credits, click here.

And remember, if things change, you can easily reschedule or cancel your bookings online up to 24 hours before the scheduled start time.

This article is divided into two sections:

  • How to book a driving lesson
  • How to book a driving test package

How to book a driving lesson

Step 1.

Log in to your learner account

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, next to your instructor’s profile picture, click Book Now.

Step 3.

The ‘Make a Booking’ screen will appear. Click Booking Type to view booking options.

Step 4.

Select 1-Hour Lesson or 2-Hour Lesson from the dropdown.

Step 5.

Under Availability Dates, select your lesson date and your lesson start time.

Step 6.

Scroll down to check the Pick Up Location. Click Save.

Step 7. (optional)

If the default pick up address needs to be changed, click edit. Update the information and then click Save.

Step 8.

As a default, the Bookings will appear and the Order Summary will be collapsed. Simply click the ^ to expand the Order Summary to view the payment amount. 

Step 9.

The order summary will now show. If you have sufficient credit in your wallet, it will use this to secure your booking. If you do not have sufficient credit, go to step 11.

Step 10.

Click Continue.

Step 11. (optional)

You will return to your Dashboard and an onscreen booking confirmation will appear. You will also be sent a confirmation email with a calendar invite.

Step 12. (optional)

If you have insufficient credit available in your wallet, your Order Summary will display the amount payable. This will be deducted from your saved payment method. 

Step 13. (optional)

Click Continue.

Step 14. (optional)

If you do not have saved payment details, select the payment method and enter the details. 

Step 15. (optional)

Scroll down to check the billing information, this will be included on your payment receipt and cannot be amended.

Step 16. (optional)

Click Pay.

Step 17. (optional)

You will return to your Dashboard and an onscreen booking confirmation will appear. You will also be sent a confirmation email with a calendar invite.

How to book a driving test package

Step 1.

Log in to your learner account

Step 2.

On your Dashboard, next to your instructor’s profile picture, click Book Now.

Step 3.

The ‘Make a Booking’ screen will appear. Click Booking Type to view booking options.

Step 4.

Select Driving Test Package from the dropdown. A warning will appear - you must book your own driving test with the local roads authority.

Step 5.

Select your Driving test location. If the test centre is not listed, it is not serviced by your selected instructor. 

Step 6.

Under Availability Dates, select your test date and your test start time. The test centre's booking system operates in 10 minute increments, ours operates in 15 minute increments. As a result, we may not show the exact test time, you simply need to book the closest test time (within 5 minutes) in our system. If the time is not selectable, your selected instructor is unavailable for your test.

Step 7.

Scroll down to check the Pick Up and Drop Off Locations. Click Save.

Step 8.

As a default, the Bookings will appear and the Order Summary will be collapsed. Simply click the ^ to expand the Order Summary to view the payment amount. 

Step 9.

The order summary will now show. If you have sufficient credit in your wallet, it will use this to secure your booking. If you do not have sufficient credit, go to step 12.

Step 10.

Click Continue.

Step 11.

You will return to your Dashboard and an onscreen booking confirmation will appear. You will also be sent a confirmation email with a calendar invite.

Step 12. (optional)

If you have insufficient credit available in your wallet, your Order Summary will display the amount payable. This will be deducted from your saved payment method. 

Step 13. (optional)

Click Continue.

Step 14. (optional)

If you do not have saved payment details, select the payment method and enter the details. 

Step 15. (optional)

Scroll down to check the billing information, this will be included on your payment receipt and cannot be amended.

Step 16. (optional)

Click Pay.

Step 17. (optional)

You will return to your Dashboard and an onscreen booking confirmation will appear. You will also be sent a confirmation email with a calendar invite.

Once you have placed your booking, it should also be visible in your Upcoming Bookings. For further instructions on how to check your booking, click here.

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Get a tax invoice.

Overview:

EzLicence is an online booking platform. Whenever you make a purchase, you will instantly receive a payment receipt.

Essentially, we collect the initial payment from you, and hold it until you complete your booking. Once you have completed your booking, we pass that full payment on to the driving instructor.

We are unable to issue a tax invoice.  Each driving instructor is an independent operator, some of whom are registered for GST and others are not. You will need to request a tax invoice directly from the driving instructor you complete your booking with.

As you are able to change driving instructors at any time, you will only be able to request an invoice for completed bookings.

This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform

You are trying to: 

Action a booking proposal sent by your instructor.

Overview:

At times, you may receive a booking proposal from your instructor.  A booking proposal is initiated by your driving instructor to proactively suggest a specific date and time for a driving lesson. There are a few different situations when your driving instructor may send you a booking proposal:

  • You need a booking when the instructor is normally closed, but they are happy to help out
  • The driving instructor has had a cancellation and can fit you in
  • The instructor has needed to reschedule one of your existing bookings
  • You are in the middle of your 1 hour booking and you both think an extra hour would be beneficial

When a booking proposal is sent, you will be notified by both email and SMS. Both options will direct you to log in to your learner account. The instructions below are divided into sections on how to:

  • Accept a booking proposal
  • Decline a booking proposal
  • Find a booking proposal

How to accept a booking proposal

Step 1.

If actioning from an email, click the Accept or reschedule → button.

Alternatively, if actioning from an SMS, click the link.

Step 2.

Log in to your learner account. 

Step 3.

Review the booking information.

Step 4.

Click ✔️ Confirm Booking button.

You will then be directed to your Dashboard and you should be able to view this new booking in your Upcoming Bookings list.

How to decline a booking proposal

Step 1.

If actioning from an email, click the Accept or reschedule → button.

Alternatively, if actioning from an SMS, click the link.

Step 2.

Log in to your learner account. 

Step 3.

Move the toggle in the top right corner to show Decline.

Step 4. 

Click x Decline Booking button.

How to find the booking proposal

If at any time you navigate from the proposal screen, you can return to the proposal at any time before it expires. There will be a notification banner at the top of your Dashboard:

Important

Each booking proposal will expire at the booking start time OR after 24hrs from when it was sent (whichever comes first).

Once you complete step 2 above, you will automatically be taken to the booking proposal. If the booking proposal has expired, you will be directed to your Dashboard with an onscreen message:

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Set the frequency of your payouts. 

Overview:

At EzLicence, we believe in empowering instructors with control over their earnings. That's why we offer flexible payment options, allowing you to choose when you want to get paid. 

Whether it's weekly, fortnightly, or monthly (4 weekly), you can select your preferred payout frequency to suit your needs and preferences. This flexibility assists you to manage your finances in a way that works best for you, providing peace of mind.

How to set your payout frequency

Step 1.

From your Dashboard, find the menu and select Banking.

Step 2.

Scroll down to the section titled Payout Frequency, click Edit.

Step 3.

Select the frequency of your payout, click Save.

Important

Your earnings will accrue until your scheduled payout.

Changing your payout frequency does come with associated processing fees. Please refer to our Payout Processing Fees Fact Sheet for further information.

All payouts are processed in an existing schedule, therefore in some instances, changing your payout frequency from weekly to fortnightly may result in a payment the following week to bring your fortnightly payments into line and then the payout will be fortnightly from then on.

You have the flexibility to modify your payout settings at any time. 

  • Changes made before 4am on Monday of the payout week will apply to that payout. 
  • Adjustments made from 4am onward on Monday will take effect for the following payout period.

Unfortunately, there are times when your driving instructor might need to cancel a booking. Life happens and there are some situations which can't be avoided. Some examples are:

  • Driving instructor is unwell
  • Someone in their immediate family is unwell and they need to provide care

When this happens, your driving instructor should make contact as soon as possible to let you know. The instructor can action either of the following:

  1. Reschedule your booking

The instructor will send you a booking proposal, this will instantly return the credit to your EzLicence wallet. Refer to our support article for more information - How to action a booking proposal

  1. Cancel your booking

The instructor can cancel the booking online, this will instantly return the credit to your EzLicence wallet. Refer to our support article for more information - How to view your booking credits

If you would prefer to change driving instructor and rebook, you can do this at any time and at no cost. Refer to our support article for more information - How to change your driving instructor

My instructor hasn’t arrive for my booking

Step 1.

Life is busy and sometimes we make a mistake, check that you have the correct date, time and location.  Refer to our support article for more information - How to check my booking

Step 2.

Make contact with your instructor, they may be just around the corner. Refer to our support article for more information - How to contact your driving instructor

Step 3.

Please lodge a dispute by following the instructions in our support article - How to lodge a dispute.

articles.title: Booking Modification Policy (Edit, Reschedule and Cancel)
articles.excerpt: Overview At EzLicence, we understand that plans can change, and flexibility is essential. This Policy provides detailed information on...
articles.content:
Overview

At EzLicence, we understand that plans can change, and flexibility is essential. This Policy provides detailed information on how, when and by whom bookings can be modified to ensure fairness and clarity.

Definitions

Booking

Reserving a time in the driving instructor’s calendar via the EzLicence platform.

Modification

Any change to a booking, including edits,  reschedules or cancellations made via the EzLicence platform.

Defining an edit

An edit means to change the details of a booking such as the duration, a pick-up or drop-off address. In order for an edit to be accepted, it must be made via the EzLicence platform.

Defining a reschedule

A reschedule means to change the date and time of a booking. In order for a reschedule to be accepted, it must be made via the EzLicence platform.

Defining a cancellation

A cancellation means to indefinitely postpone, call off, revoke, or annul a booking. In order for a cancellation to be accepted must be made via the EzLicence platform.

Who can modify a booking?

A booking can be modified by 3 parties:

  1. Learner driver or secondary account holder
  2. Driving instructor
  3. EzLicence

Modification Classifications

Voluntary modification

Made by a user via the EzLicence platform.

Examples:

  • A learner user logs into their account and reschedules a booking.
  • A learner user logs into their account and cancels a booking.
  • A driving instructor user logs into their account and cancels a booking.
  • A driving instructor user logs into their account and reschedules a booking.

Involuntary modification

An implied change due to inactivity, a booking error or a third party. All involuntary modifications are considered cancellations.

Examples:

  • A ‘no show’ where learner does not arrive at the pick up location on the date and time of their booking
  • The learner contact phone number or the pick up address were incomplete or incorrect
  • A learner has a driving test booking cancelled by the licencing authority due to incomplete paperwork
  • A learner does not hold a valid and legal licence to drive as required by state legislation
  • A driving instructor ‘no show’ where the instructor does not arrive at the pick up location within 15 minutes of the start time of the booking

Modification Windows

A modification can occur in two time frames;

  1. Before the cancellation window 

Modifications made up to 24 hours before the scheduled booking time.

  1. After the cancellation window 

Modifications made less than 24 hours before the scheduled booking time.

Modification Rules

Before Cancellation Window

Full booking credit returned to the learner’s EzLicence wallet without incurring any fees.

After Cancellation Window

A cancellation fee equal to the booking cost will be paid to the driving instructor.

Examples of Modifications

Booking modification = edit

Booking modification = reschedule

Booking modification = cancellation

Booking modification = 'no show'

In the event of a ‘no show’ every attempt must be made to contact the other party,  this includes: 

  • Phone call 
  • SMS 
  • Waiting out the front of the pick up address

OR

  • Knocking on the front door (where complete address is available)

Dispute Resolution

Process: Contact your instructor first. If unresolved after 48 hours, submit a dispute to EzLicence Customer Support within 72 hours. For further information, refer to our support article - How to lodge a dispute.

Review: Disputes will be reviewed by our support team, and a decision will be communicated within 7 working days.

Outcome: If the dispute is resolved in favour of the learner, any charges applied will be credited or refunded appropriately.

Changes to This Policy

EzLicence reserves the right to update or modify this policy at any time. Changes will take effect immediately upon posting on our website. Continued use of the platform after any changes signifies acceptance of our updated policy.

Contact Information

For any queries or assistance regarding our Booking Modification Policy, please submit a support request.

articles.title: Driving Instructor Cancellation Policy
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Understand the...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand the EzLicence Booking Modification Policy

Overview:

At EzLicence, we understand that flexibility is essential when plans change. Our Booking Modification Policy provides clear guidelines for editing, rescheduling, or cancelling bookings. This policy ensures that all modifications are handled fairly and transparently, whether initiated by the learner, the instructor, or EzLicence. By defining the processes and time frames for making changes, we aim to maintain a smooth and equitable experience for everyone involved.

If you need to modify a booking, please keep the following in mind:

  • All modifications must be made through the EzLicence platform
  • Providing as much notice as possible to the learner ensures a better experience for everyone
  • Maintain clear, concise, and professional communication at all times
  • Whenever possible, aim to reschedule a booking rather than cancelling

To view the Booking Modification Policy, click here.

For more detailed guidance on managing bookings, you may find the following support articles helpful:

  • How to reschedule a booking from your calendar
  • How to cancel a booking from your calendar
  • How to reverse a payment
  • How to handle a learner 'no show'

These articles include step-by-step instructions to help you efficiently manage your bookings and ensure a smooth experience for both you and your learners.

Misuse

Misuse of booking modifications by Instructors includes, but is not limited to:

  • Cancelling more than 3 times on the same learner
  • Cancelling bookings in favour of a private learner
  • Cancelling due to a perceived low payment rate for a booking
  • Asking a learner to cancel on your behalf
  • Repeated instances of last-minute cancellations
  • Cancelling a learner’s first booking without a valid reason. Below are some examples of a valid reason:
    • You have issues with your car preventing you from delivering bookings;
    • You or a member of your immediate family experienced a medical emergency;
    • Your doctor has advised that you are unable to work;
    • Severe weather warnings

Consequences

Driving instructors found to be misusing the system or engaging in discriminatory cancellation behaviour may face disciplinary actions, including warnings, temporary suspension, or removal from the platform.

Overview:

We’re here for you. Disputes can arise due to miscommunication, misunderstanding, or breaches of policies. While our policies are robust, we are open to reviewing cases where you feel there may have been mistakes or exceptional circumstances. For your convenience, please refer to the following policies:

  • Learner Cancellation Policy
  • Refund Policy
  • Review Policy
  • Learner Terms and Conditions
  • Driving Instructor Code of Conduct

In the first instance, please raise your concerns directly with your instructor. If the issue remains unresolved, submit your dispute to EzLicence Support within 72 hours. 

How to lodge a dispute

Step 1.

Contact your instructor directly to try to resolve the issue directly. For further information, please refer to our support article - How to contact your driving instructor

Step 2.

Collect the following information:

  • Booking ID
  • Date and time of booking
  • Supporting evidence (including photos and screenshots) 

For further information, please refer to our support article - How to view your booking history.

Step 3.

Lodge your dispute, click here and follow these steps: 

  1. Click Submit a Request
  2. Choose "I am a Learner Driver"
  3. Provide your details
  4. Choose "Lodge a dispute"
  5. Please provide as much context, detail and evidence as you can to support why you disagree with the charge
  6. Click Submit

Important

You only need to submit your request once, you will receive a confirmation via email which will include a reference number. Submitting your request multiple times will only slow down the resolution process.

Our team will respond via your support request, please keep your eye out for our response as sometimes it can land in your junk / spam folder.

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Keep track of your learners and their progress.

Overview:

EzLicence provides a centralised hub within each instructor account where driving instructors can efficiently handle various aspects of their student interactions. In this section, instructors can:

  1. View learner details - access information about each learner, including their age and contact details.
  1. Send bookings - initiate and manage lesson bookings directly from the platform, simplifying the scheduling process for both instructors and learners.
  1. Record progress notes - make and store detailed progress notes, enabling instructors to track learner achievements or areas that need improvement.
  1. View upcoming and historical bookings - easily monitor and manage learners' upcoming lessons as well as review past bookings, creating a seamless and organised record.

How to manage your learners

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2. 

From your Dashboard, find the menu and select Learners. This will show you a list of all learners who have an upcoming booking or have completed a booking with you in the past 6 months.

Step 3.

The list is in alphabetical order, you can either scroll to locate your learner or you can enter a learner’s first name or phone number in the Search field.

Step 4.

Step 5.

articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand the information on your tax invoice. 

Overview:

Your tax invoice gives you a detailed breakdown of the payments you’ve made to EzLicence, which can be useful when completing your tax return.

You will automatically be issued a tax invoice each time you receive a payout. It will be issued via email and you can also access it at any time via your instructor account.

At the top of the tax invoice:

  • Business information: Your registered business information
  • Date: The day the payment was made, this may not be the date you receive it
  • ID: A unique reference number for the payment
  • Amount: The total amount deducted from your payout

In the middle of the tax invoice, itemised by booking with the GST separately displayed:

Marketplace learner

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (Booking ID#, type and duration)
  • Learner Discounts: Any discounts received by the learner due to purchase in bulk
  • Booking Service Fee: The amount paid to EzLicence for the booking

Invited learner

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (Booking ID#, type and duration)
  • Learner Discounts: Any discounts received by the learner for purchasing in bulk
  • Invited Learner Fee: The administration fee paid to EzLicence for invited learners

Reversals

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (Booking ID#, type and duration)
  • Booking Service Fee: A reversal of the amount previously deducted and paid to EzLicence for the booking

At the bottom of the tax invoice:

  • Payout Processing Fee deducted for this payout
  • Total: This is the total amount paid to EzLicence for the payout period (incl GST)
  • GST included: The total amount GST included in the payout
  • EzLicence's business information: Our business address, company number and contact details will be listed

Your transaction report helps you keep track of the payments you've received for lessons. It gives you a clear picture of your earnings and transactions over a specific period.

You will automatically be issued a transaction report each time you receive a payout. It will be issued via email and you can also access it at any time via your instructor account. For further information, click here.

There is a lot of information on your transaction report, so let’s break it down section by section.

At the top of the transaction report:

  • Business information: Your registered business information
  • Date: The day the payment was made, this may not be the date you receive it
  • ID: A unique reference number for the payment
  • Amount: The total amount paid into your bank account

In the middle of the transaction report, itemised by booking:

Marketplace learner

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (date, type and duration)
  • Amount Paid: The total amount paid for the lesson
  • Learner Discounts: Any discounts received by the learner due to purchase in bulk
  • Booking Service Fee: The amount paid to EzLicence for the booking
  • Net Payment: The net amount paid to you for each booking completed

Invited learner

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (date, type and duration)
  • Amount Paid: The total amount paid for the lesson
  • Learner Discounts: Any discounts received by the learner for purchasing in bulk
  • Invited Learner Fee: The administration fee paid to EzLicence for invited learners
  • Net Payment: The net amount paid to you for each booking completed

Reversals

  • Student Name: The first name of the student who made the payment
  • Booking Details: A summary of the lesson (date, type and duration)
  • Amount Paid: The total deducted from your payout for the lesson previously paid to you
  • Booking Service Fee: The amount previously deducted and paid to EzLicence for the booking
  • Net Payment: The net amount deducted from your payout for a previously paid lesson

At the bottom of the transaction report:

  • Payment Processing Fee: The amount charged to process this payment

articles.title: Private Learner Fact Sheet
articles.excerpt: ‘Private Learner' means your private learners and EzLicence marketplace learners can all be managed through your EzLicence Dashboard. For...
articles.content:
‘Private Learner' means your private learners and EzLicence marketplace learners can all be managed through your EzLicence Dashboard.

For a 2.4% (incl GST) admin fee per booking, give your private learners the same flexible and convenient service that your EzLicence marketplace learners currently enjoy. Use our technology to convert more learners and reduce time spent on communication, payments, bookkeeping and other administration. 

Some important considerations are outlined below:

  1. Send an Invitation:
    Use the “Invite Learner” option from your dashboard. Enter the learner (or parent) mobile phone number. We will send them an SMS with a unique link directly to your profile.  You can also access this link via your instructor account under the ‘My Profile & Vehicle’ menu. Share it via the platform of your choice (Facebook, WhatsApp, website etc) to watch your business grow.
  2. Phone Number Guarantee:
    If the learner signs up using the invitation, we’ll ensure they cannot register as an EzLicence Marketplace learner using the same phone number. This means you’ll pay the reduced rate of 2.4% per booking.
  3. No Guarantee of Link Usage:
    Although we encourage learners to use the invitation link with a follow up SMS, we cannot guarantee they will sign up through it.
  4. Cookie Tracking:
    When a learner clicks the invitation link, we’ll attempt to place a cookie on their browser to track their signup for 24 hours. Keep in mind that we cannot guarantee the cookie will function as intended due to factors out of our control, such us - individual device settings, user permissions, or browser behaviour. Inviting via our SMS system is always the easiest way for us to attribute the invitation.
  5. Each booking will incur a 2.4% (incl GST) admin fee. This includes 0.65% to cover EzLicence’s administration costs and 1.75% to cover learner card processing.

This feature offers a great way to ensure all your learners enjoy a seamless booking experience and maximise your earnings all while running an efficient business. For further information, click here.

Disputing Marketplace Service Fees

  • If a Learner signs up via the marketplace:
    If you believe a learner who signed up through the Marketplace was initially invited by you, you can dispute the Marketplace service fees. Please lodge a dispute via a support request and provide evidence of your invitation to support your claim.
  • Investigation and Resolution:
    We will investigate the situation to find a fair resolution, which may involve reimbursing service fees at the end of the learner’s journey.

articles.title: Your wallet explained
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Understanding your EzLicence...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understanding your EzLicence wallet.

Overview:

Your EzLicence wallet makes managing your payments and bookings easy and convenient. Here’s how it works:

  • Adding Funds: You can add credit to your wallet at any time using your preferred payment method.
  • Using the Wallet: When you book, the price for the booking will automatically be deducted from your wallet balance.
  • Top-Ups: If your wallet doesn't have enough credit, you can top it up before confirming a booking.
  • Reversals: Any eligible reversals from a cancellation will go back into your wallet, ready for future use.
  • Checking Your Balance: You can always view your wallet balance and transaction history through your account.

The wallet system ensures faster bookings and fewer payment hassles—so you can focus on learning to drive!

Wallet Usage Details

What price will I pay?

The amount deducted from your wallet will reflect the instructor’s price at the time you made your booking. The good news is that even if the instructor’s price increases later, you’ve locked in the lower rate! While there may be times when an instructor lowers their price after your booking, rest assured that your payment will stay the same as the amount you originally secured—providing you with consistency and peace of mind.

First In, First Out (FIFO) Rule

Our wallet system follows the First In, First Out (FIFO) rule. This means that the oldest credits in your wallet will be used first for payments. By automatically using your earliest credits, we help ensure that nothing goes to waste and you stay on top of your balance efficiently.

Bonus Credits

If you are eligible for a discount, this will be applied as bonus credits added to your wallet. 

Example:

  • The instructor’s price is $100 per hour
  • You purchase 10 hours with a 10% discount
  • You pay $900, and $100 is added to your wallet as bonus credit

When making bookings, both cash and bonus credits are used proportionately.

Example:

  • For a booking priced at $100 per hour,  $90 will be deducted from your cash balance, and $10 from your bonus credit balance.

This ensures you get the full benefit of your discount with each booking!

If you’ve received bonus credits as part of a promotion or discount, please note that only the cash amount you added to your wallet can be refunded. Bonus credits are non-refundable and meant to be used toward future bookings. This way, any bonuses you earn stay with you to help make the most of your driving journey!

Enjoy the convenience of having everything you need to manage your business in one place.

How to invite your learner to book into your calendar

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2. 

From the Dashboard, select Invite Learner.

Enter your learner’s name and mobile number.

How to book your private learners

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Under the section titled Learner Pickup Postcode, select the learner’s pickup location. You will only be able to choose postcodes that are listed in your service area. If you need to make changes or further information, click here.

articles.title: VORT vs CBT&A Driving Lessons in South Australia
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if:  You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Understand the types of instructor...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Learner User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand the types of instructor qualifications in South Australia.

Overview:

When it comes to choosing an instructor, we believe that choice and transparency is key. At EzLicence, we work hard to give you all the options to make the best decisions for you, that includes different types of Driving Instructors.  In South Australia, there are two types of instructor qualifications; 

  1. VORT; and 
  2. CBT&A (commonly referred to as logbook) 

Choosing between the VORT or CBT&A (Logbook) learning pathway is not something that you should do without understanding the differences between the two. In the following sections, we break down the differences to help you make the right decision for you.

VORT

Known as the “Vehicle On Road Driving Test (VORT)”, VORT is the traditional method for securing your Adelaide probationary driving licence. 

The VORT involves sitting a practical driving test with an authorised examiner. This is not the same person who delivers your driving lessons.

The VORT tests your skills to ensure that you are a safe and knowledgeable driver. The test typically takes around 45 minutes to complete.

You will need to complete five slow-speed manoeuvers: a U-turn, a 3-point turn, a reverse parallel park, an angled park, and a hill start.  

The driving part of the test will test your skills in changing lanes, navigating traffic lights and roundabouts, responding to signage, and interacting with your car and other road users.

You’ll need to score at least 90% to pass the VORT test and cannot break any road laws while completing the drive. If you do breach a road law, you will immediately fail the VORT, and your test will not continue. 

The main advantage of VORT is that it can be quite quick and a relatively cheap way to get your licence, especially if you are already a confident driver.

In South Australia, the VORT test is facilitated by private assessors, as a result you are unable to book this via our website. If you do book lessons with a driving instructor, they will certainly be able to help book this in for you.

CBT&A (Logbook)

The Competency Based Training and Assessment pathway is commonly called the “Adelaide Logbook” method because you will work through a logbook of 30 tasks with an accredited instructor. 

The main benefit of the Adelaide logbook method is that it allows you to work through the tasks at your own pace and spend more time on areas that require further practice. 

Task 30 is similar to the VORT Test, but it is conducted by the driving instructor who has taught you. 

Once you have completed all tasks, this will be recorded in your Drivers Companion with a certificate of competency issued. You will then be eligible for your P1 licence.

It is important to note that CBT&A (Logbook) instructors are often booked out months in advance, so this may factor this into your decision of which pathway to choose.

All driving instructors in SA are qualified to deliver VORT lessons. Not all instructors are qualified to deliver CBT&A (Logbook) lessons. Our website will show which qualifications they have on their profile page.

You can also use the ‘Lesson’ filter at the top of the page to narrow your search.

articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.You are trying to: Understand how profile...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

You are trying to: 

Understand how profile badges work and how to earn them.

Overview:
Profile badges help you stand out in search results and build trust with learners. They are awarded based on your performance and profile details. Badges can increase your visibility and help you get more bookings.

Profile Badges and How They’re Awarded

We currently offer three badges:

  • Top Instructor:
    • Average rating 4.8+ and at least 30 reviews
    • This badge stays on your profile as long as you meet the rating and review requirements
  • Great Value:
    • Rating of 4.6+ and competitive pricing (lowest 30% for that search)
    • This badge changes based on each learner’s search
  • High Demand:
    • Instructors that received the most booking hours in the past 7 days
    • This badge changes based on each learner’s search

Note: Great Value and High Demand badges are shown based on location and transmission type. This means you are compared to other instructors in the same search, not across the whole country.

How to Earn Badges

Top Instructor

  • Deliver a great learner experience: be on time and avoid last-minute cancellations
  • Ask for reviews after lessons

Great Value

  • Check your pricing often and keep it competitive
  • Maintain a strong rating (4.6+) by delivering quality lessons

High Demand

  • Show as many upcoming time slots as possible in your calendar
  • Propose bookings to learners who plan to rebook so you lock in their next lesson early
  • Upsell test packages to your learners to secure more hours in advance
  • Add more time slots during busy periods if possible
  • Avoid cancellations and reschedules

articles.title: How to create a recurring event
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to: Send multiple recurring...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.
 

You are trying to: 

Send multiple recurring bookings to your learner.
 

Overview:

Recurring events are ideal when you have learners who prefer to take lessons on a regular, ongoing basis. Using this feature helps you stay organised and maintain a steady schedule.

You might use recurring events when:

  • A learner wants to book the same day and time each week.

  • You want to reserve a regular slot for a learner to help them stay consistent.

  • You’d like to fill your calendar in advance to ensure predictable income and availability.

  • You want to reduce admin time by avoiding the need to manually create each individual booking.

Recurring events make it easier for both you and your learners to plan ahead, minimise scheduling conflicts, and support consistent learning progress.

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to navigate your calendar. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to create a recurring event 

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar

Step 3.

Click anywhere on the calendar to Create an Event OR in the top right, click Create Event

Step 4.

In this example we are creating a recurring event for an EzLicence Learner. Click 'New Booking'.

Step 5.

Click Select a learner. A list of all learners who have completed a booking with you in the past 6 months or have an upcoming booking will be displayed.

Step 6.

Click a Booking type, the options are a 1-hour driving lesson, 2-hour driving lesson or a driving test package.

Step 7.

Click and select a day and select a time from the drop down menus. 

Step 8.  

To create booking proposals for multiple recurring events at once, click the drop-down arrow under Recurrence to select your recurrence type. The options are Do not repeat, Daily, Weekly on Friday. 

Next select the number of Occurrences, then click Review.

Step 9.

Once complete, click Send Booking Proposal. Your proposed booking/s will now appear in your calendar as a light grey event.

Step 10.

You can also view and manage your Pending Proposals from your Dashboard.

What’s Next?

The learner will receive a notification, which will be sent via SMS and email. Learners can accept or reject the proposal from their learner account.

Booking proposals will expire at the booking start time OR after 24 hrs from when it was sent (whichever comes first).

Once it is accepted by the learner, it will change to a yellow confirmed booking.

This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.
 

You are trying to: 

Work outside your normal hours and open up extra hours on an ad-hoc basis.
 

Overview:

The extra hours feature allows instructors to open additional availability on an ad-hoc basis without changing their regular schedule. This is useful when you want to offer extra lesson times for a particular day, such as fitting in a new learner, accommodating a reschedule, or taking on more bookings during busy periods.


You might use Extra Hours when:

  • Fitting in new learners who can’t find a suitable time within your standard availability.

  • Accommodating reschedules that don’t work within your usual hours.

  • Taking on additional bookings during particularly busy periods.

  • Opening up short-term availability when you know you’ll be free on a specific day.

  • Covering gaps created by cancellations or unexpected changes to your schedule.


By adding extra hours, you can temporarily extend your availability for specific dates while keeping your standard weekly hours unchanged. This gives you greater flexibility and helps learners find suitable times to book with you.

How to open Extra Hours  

Step 1.

Log in to your instructor account.

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar.

Step 3.

Click anywhere on the calendar to Create an Event OR in the top right, click Create Event.

Step 4.

In this example we are creating extra open hours. Click 'Extra Open Hours'.

Step 5.

Select the date you want to create extra open hours.

Step 6.

Select the start time and end time you want to open. These hours will be added on top of your regular availability for that day. 

Step 7.

Click Save to apply the extra hours to your calendar 

Step 8.

Once complete, your calendar will display your Extra Open Hours in purple, showing the additional availability learners can book.

1. Promotion Period

Entries open 12:00am Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) on 1 December 2025 (10:30am Adelaide time (ACDT) on 1 December 2025) and close 11:59pm UTC on 28 February 2026 (10:29am Adelaide time (ACDT) on 1 March 2026) (“Promotion Period”).

2. Eligibility

2.1 For the purposes of these Terms, a “Promotion” means the giveaway conducted by the Promoter as described in these Terms, including all associated entry, draw and prize fulfilment processes.

2.1 Entry is open to Australian residents, excluding residents of the Northern Territory.

2.2 Entrants must hold, or be eligible to hold, a learner’s permit or driver’s licence in their state/territory of residence.

2.3 The following are ineligible: employees, directors and management of the Promoter and the agencies/companies associated with this Promotion, and their immediate family members.

2.4 For the purposes of these Terms, a “Secondary Account Holder” means a person (such as a parent, guardian, or other authorised individual) who has created or is registered on an account for the purpose of managing, paying for, or booking driving lessons on behalf of a learner driver.

2.5 Where an account is registered in the name of a Secondary Account Holder but used to book lessons for a learner driver, the learner driver will be deemed to be the entrant in this Promotion, provided they otherwise meet the eligibility requirements in these Terms.

2.6 Where the learner driver (entrant) does not have contact details recorded in their account, the Promoter will, where possible, contact the Secondary Account Holder associated with that learner’s account for the purposes of winner notification, verification, and prize fulfilment. The entrant authorises the Promoter to contact the Secondary Account holder for these purposes. Where no Secondary Account Holder is recorded, the Promoter will take reasonable steps to contact the entrant using any contact details available in the account. Notification to a Secondary Account Holder, where applicable, will be deemed notification to the entrant for the purposes of this Promotion.

3. How to Enter

3.1 During the Promotion Period, an eligible entrant will receive entries by completing eligible bookings through the Promoter’s website/platform for the eligible booking types listed below. 

3.2 “Completed” means the lesson or test package booking has been delivered and marked complete (not merely booked or paid). Cancelled, rescheduled, reverted or refunded credits do not qualify.

3.3 Entries are automatically recorded against the entrant’s account once the qualifying lesson or test package is completed.

3.4 No other methods of entry apply. There are no bonus entry actions other than those listed below.

3.5 Purchase of lessons and packages is for the primary purpose of obtaining driving instruction. Any entries awarded are incidental to that purchase.

3.6 Each entry constitutes a separate chance to win and all entries have an equal, random chance of selection in the draw.

3.7 For the avoidance of doubt, the completion timestamp and qualifying status recorded in the Promoter’s system will be conclusive evidence of eligibility and entry allocation (except in the case of manifest error).

 

Entry Allocation Table

4. Verification

4.1 The Promoter may require entrants and/or the winner to provide proof of identity, age and eligibility and evidence of completed bookings.

4.2 It is the entrant’s responsibility to ensure their contact details are accurate and current.

5. Draw

5.1 The winner will be determined by a random electronic draw conducted by the Promoter at 10:00am Adelaide time (ACDT) on 6 March 2026 (11:30pm UTC, 5 March 2026) (“Draw”) at 217-219 Flinders St, Adelaide SA 5000.

5.2 The Draw will use a computerised random selection process (randomdraws.com.au (via Trade Promotions & Lotteries Pty Ltd)) producing a verifiable audit log showing date, time, algorithm, and selected entry. 

5.3 The Draw process will be overseen by an authorised representative of the Promoter and, where required by law, an independent scrutineer.

5.4 All entries in the Draw will have an equal, random chance of winning.

5.5 The Promoter will retain the draw log, entry records and winner verification records for at least 3 years and make them available to regulators upon request.

6. Prize

6.1 One (1) prize only: 2025 Mazda 2 G15 Evolve hatch (base model) — Skyactiv-G 1.5L Petrol, FWD, 6-speed automatic — RRP valued up to AUD $28,990 including 12 months registration, 12 months compulsory third party (CTP) insurance, stamp duty, other applicable statutory charges, dealer delivery and administration charges (“Prize”).

6.2 The vehicle specification and colour are subject to availability. If the specified model becomes unavailable, the Promoter may substitute an equivalent vehicle of equal or higher value and comparable specification (subject to regulatory approval if required).

6.3 Order & Delivery: The Prize will be ordered after the winner is verified to ensure the vehicle is purchased and registered in the winner’s state/territory. Delivery is subject to manufacturer/dealer lead times and may take up to six (6) months (or longer if impacted by supply constraints outside the Promoter’s control). 

6.4 On-road costs set out in 6.1 are covered by the Promoter. The winner is responsible for any transport/accommodation costs to collect the vehicle and for insurance (beyond CTP), fuel and ongoing costs.

6.5 The Promoter will make reasonable efforts to arrange local purchase and delivery in the winner’s state/territory.

6.6 Total prize pool: up to AUD $28,990.

6.7. Prize value is the recommended retail price (RRP) inclusive of applicable taxes and charges, accurate at the time of publishing. Actual value may vary due to pricing changes or availability. No compensation will be provided for any variation, subject to clause 6.2 (substitution) and applicable law.

7. Notification & Publication

7.1 The provisional winner will be notified by email and phone within two (2) business days of the Draw. 

7.2 Where the winning entrant does not have contact details recorded on their EzLicence account, the Promoter will notify the Secondary Account Holder linked to the account. Notification to the Secondary Account Holder will constitute notification to the entrant.

7.3 The winner’s initial, surname and postcode will be published on ezlicence.com.au within seven (7) business days of the Draw and may be published on the Promoter’s social channels (as required or permitted by law).

7.4 If the winner is under 18 years of age or is otherwise unable to register the vehicle, the Promoter may, in its discretion, award the Prize to the winner’s parent or legal guardian to be registered in their name for the benefit of the winner (as permitted by law). Their parent/guardian must hold a valid driver’s licence to lawfully drive the vehicle.

8. Prize Claim & Unclaimed Prize Draw

8.1 The winner must claim the Prize within two (2) months of the Draw and complete all verification and acceptance steps.

8.2 If the Prize is unclaimed or the winner is deemed ineligible, an Unclaimed Prize Draw will be conducted at the same location using the same method at 10:00am Adelaide time on 6 May 2026 (11:30pm UTC, 5 May 2026). The unclaimed winner will be notified and published as per clause 7.

9. Entrants Under 18 Years of Age

9.1 Entrants aged under 18 may enter. However, if an entrant under 18 is drawn as the winner, the Promoter may require the entrant’s parent/legal guardian to provide written consent for the entrant to receive the Prize and to sign any documentation required by the Promoter (including releases/indemnities).

9.2 If the required consent/documents are not provided within the time specified, the Prize may be forfeited and awarded to another entrant pursuant to clause 8.

9.3 Registration of the Prize will be handled in accordance with clause 7.4.

10. General

10.1 Entries must be received and recorded during the Promotion Period. The Promoter is not responsible for technical issues that delay/prevent lesson completion from being recorded, including lesson cancellation or reschedule, except to the extent caused by the Promoter’s negligence.

10.2 Incomplete, fraudulent or non-compliant entries may be deemed invalid.

10.3 The Promoter may verify any entry and disqualify entrants who interfere with or tamper with the entry process.

10.4 The Promoter may vary, suspend or cancel the Promotion if it becomes unable to proceed as contemplated due to circumstances outside the Promoter’s reasonable control, subject to any written directions from regulators.

10.5 Liability: Nothing in these T&Cs limits any non-excludable guarantees under the Australian Consumer Law. Subject to those rights, the Promoter is not liable for any loss or damage (including personal injury) suffered arising from (i) any failure to perform or delay in performance of its obligations under these Terms, to the extent that the failure or delay is caused by any act, omission, event, or circumstance outside of the reasonable control of the Promoter; or (ii) taking the Prize, except to the extent caused by the Promoter’s negligence.

10.6 No transfer or exchange of the Prize permitted (except as set out in 6.2).

10.7 All draw, entry, and winner records will be retained for at least 3 years (or longer if required by law) and made available to regulators upon request.

11. Privacy

11.1 The Promoter collects personal information to conduct the Promotion and award the Prize. If the information requested is not provided, the entrant may be ineligible.

11.2 Personal information will be handled in accordance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Promoter’s Privacy Policy available at ezlicence.com.au.

11.3 Entrants consent to their personal information being used for administering the Promotion (including winner publication) and for marketing where they have expressly opted-in. All marketing communications will include a functional unsubscribe facility and entrants can opt out of marketing at any time.

11.4 Where an entrant’s contact details are incomplete, the entrant consents to the Promoter using and disclosing personal information of a Secondary Account Holder associated with the entrant’s account for the limited purposes of winner notification, verification and prize fulfilment.

12. Governing Law

These T&Cs are governed by the laws of South Australia (SA) and each entrant submits to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of SA courts.

articles.title: How to set up Google Calendar 2-way sync
articles.excerpt: This will be useful if: You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform. You are trying to:  Connect your Google...
articles.content:
This will be useful if: 

You are a Driving Instructor User of the EzLicence booking platform.

 

You are trying to: 

  • Connect your Google Calendar to your EzLicence calendar

  • Enable two-way sync for the first time

  • Reconnect your calendar after a failed or expired connection

  • Keep your availability accurate across both calendar

 

Overview:

EzLicence’s Google Calendar sync helps keep your availability and lesson bookings accurate across both calendars. This guide walks you through how to activate two-way sync so your schedule stays consistent, up to date, and easy to manage. Use this article to ensure your calendar remains accurate, secure, and running smoothly. 

Video Walkthrough

This video will walk you through how to set up Google 2-way sync. You are able to adjust the playback speed in the settings menu  ⚙️.

How to activate and mange two way Google Calendar Sync 
 

Step 1.

Log into your instructor account.
 

Step 2.

From the menu, select Calendar
 

Step 3.

Click on the wheel icon, click Connect your Calendar


Step 4.

The Connect your calendar 2-2ay sync pop up box will appear. You will see Google Calendar, Click on Connect. 
 

Step 5.

Click on the google account you would like to sync with EzLicence. Click Continue.


Step 6.

Review the privacy policy and terms of service, if you agree, click Allow.  Select the calendar you want EzLicence to read. Wait for confirmation, Sync will finish in a few seconds. A notification will confirm when your sync is active.

For further information, please refer to our Google 2-Way Sync Frequently Asked Questions here
